datasources.description: name: Free-text task or work-item description from ClickUp, often embedding lot number and work scope (e.g. '2936 entryway has a small 12" nook...'). Used to infer phase/lot/task identity when structured fields are absent.

Phase: Intended phase identifier, extracted or normalized from the name field during ClickUp import. Populated sparsely; null values indicate phase could not be reliably parsed from the task description.
Lot: Intended lot identifier, extracted or normalized from the name field during ClickUp import. Populated sparsely; null values indicate lot could not be reliably parsed from the task description.

Task: Intended task classification or sequence number, extracted or normalized from the name field during ClickUp import. Populated sparsely; null values indicate task structure could not be reliably parsed from the description.

top_level_parent_id: Opaque ClickUp task identifier linking to a parent task hierarchy. Used to reconstruct parent-child task relationships within the ClickUp workspace for operational dependency tracking.
name: Task title or work-item label (e.g., 'H MF 31 Spec', 'H MF 76 Walls'). Encodes abbreviated lot/phase reference and work phase; primary human-readable task identifier in ClickUp.

status: ClickUp task status value from an earlier export schema (predates current `api-upload.csv`). Contains operational stage labels like 'walk stage' and 'Open' representing task lifecycle states. This field is part of a reference structure document and is not actively staged into v2 canonical tables — the current `api-upload.csv` is the active ClickUp source.
date_created: ISO 8601 date when the ClickUp task was first created. Marks the earliest operational record of work-item initiation within the task system.
date_updated: ISO 8601 date of the most recent task modification (status, assignee, date fields, etc.). Tracks operational currency; does not indicate task completion.

date_closed: Intended field for task closure date; observed as consistently empty (all null). Likely deprecated in favor of date_done and actual_c_of_o; retain for schema completeness but expect no data.
date_done: ISO 8601 date when task was marked complete or done within ClickUp. Sparse coverage (~22.77% on lot-tagged subset); indicates operational task completion independent of financial close.
assignee_username: ClickUp user name assigned responsibility for the task (e.g., 'Marcos'). Operational accountability signal; used for work routing and task ownership tracking.
priority: Task priority indicator; observed as consistently empty (all null). Field defined in schema but not populated in current export; ignore for v2.1.
due_date: ISO 8601 target completion date for the task. Sparse (~45.54% on lot-tagged subset); used to track projected work deadlines and schedule alignment.
start_date: Intended field for task start date; observed as consistently empty (all null). Field defined but unpopulated; defer to date_created and due_date for timeline.
projected_close_date: ISO 8601 forecasted closing date for the lot/vertical work. Sparse (~4.76% on lot-tagged subset); forward-looking operational estimate distinct from actual_c_of_o.

subdivision: Community/project name where the lot resides (lot-tagged subset only, ~21% of all tasks). Nullable; requires crosswalk via canonical_project mapping (e.g., 'Aarowhead' → 'Arrowhead Springs').
lot_num: Lot number identifier within the subdivision (lot-tagged subset only). Nullable; paired with subdivision to form lot-identity for joining to canonical_lot via 3-tuple discipline.
cancelled_date: Intended field for task cancellation date; observed as consistently empty (all null). Deprecated or unused; ignore for v2.1.
cancelled: Boolean flag indicating task cancellation status; observed as consistently empty (all null). Deprecated or unused; ignore for v2.1.
close_date: Intended field for task close date; observed as consistently empty (all null). Likely superseded by date_done and actual_c_of_o; retain for schema completeness but expect no data.
closed: Boolean flag indicating task closure; observed as consistently empty (all null). Deprecated or unused; ignore for v2.1.

actual_c_of_o: ISO 8601 actual certificate-of-occupancy date for the lot (vertical completion milestone). Sparse (~22.77% on lot-tagged subset); operational signal corroborating inventory closing_date.
C_of_O: Boolean flag indicating C_of_O achieved (true/false or null). Sparse; operational confirmation pairing with actual_c_of_o date to mark vertical-completion stage transition.
sold_date: ISO 8601 date when the lot was sold to buyer. Sparse; operational signal corroborating inventory sale_date; used to anchor SOLD_NOT_CLOSED state in lot lifecycle.
sold: Boolean flag indicating lot sold (true/false or null). Sparse; operational confirmation pairing with sold_date to mark transition from FINISHED_LOT to SOLD_NOT_CLOSED.

datasources.table_name: Collateral Dec2025 01 Claude.xlsx - Cost to Complete Summary
datasources.description: Unnamed: 8: Formatted dollar total column, type string; contains cost-to-complete rollup figures as text (e.g. '$61,318,045 ', '$421,443,000 ', '$482,761,045 ') with trailing whitespace. Represents phase-level or aggregate cost-to-complete summary from the Collateral Report.
Customer/Job/Phase: Community, project, or phase identifier (e.g. 'Ammon Idaho', 'Parkway Fields E-1'). Maps operational names to collateral inventory tracking.

132-187 · Inventory Asset: Balance-sheet inventory asset account (GL code 132-187) carrying lot-cost basis in USD. Represents the recorded asset value of land and development costs capitalized to inventory per QB/GL. Right-aligned numeric string with spacing; parse as decimal.

TOTAL: Sum of all inventory asset accounts for the community/phase. Matches or echoes the 132-187 column in this sample; may include other GL accounts if present in full export. Numeric string; parse as decimal.

Alloc GL Account: Allocation GL account code and label (e.g. '132-127 · Parkway E1', '132-128 · Parkway E2'). The QB subsidiary ledger account to which lot costs are posted for collateral tracking. Used internally by lender for cost allocation by phase.
Project: Canonical community/project name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs, Harmony). Source of truth for horizontal developer identity and lot-cost rollup hierarchies across operational and GL systems.

Phase or plat identifier (numeric, e.g., 123). Combines with Project and LotNo. to form the 3-tuple join key required to avoid Harmony-style lot collisions (~$6.75M risk). Phase fill is 100% in Lot Data, unlike GL feeds.
ProdType: Product type classification (SFR, MF, etc.). Drives allocation workbook breakouts and product-mix analysis; used in per-lot expected-cost calculations.

HorzSeller: Horizontal developer entity code (BCPD, BCPBL, ASD, BCPI). Authority source for canonical_entity derivation; identifies who the land-development seller is in the buyer/seller relationship.

HorzCustomer: Vertical builder entity code (BCP, Lennar, EXT, Church). Paired with HorzSeller to define the horizontal-to-vertical transaction; feeds entity identity and scope (BCPD-only filtering via Guardrail 1).
LotNo.: Lot number within the phase (numeric; normalized to strip leading zeros and .0 decimals). Combined with Project and Phase to form the 3-tuple canonical_lot_id join key.

LotArea(sqft): Lot area in square feet (string with formatting, e.g., '6,129 '). Physical lot-size metric; used in collateral advance-rate models and per-lot allocation weighting when range-row allocation method is confirmed.

LotCount: Count of lots in the record (typically 1 for individual lots). Used to reconstruct per-lot cost denominators in allocation workbooks; especially critical for planned phases with aggregate lot counts.
SHComb: Concatenated project+phase+lot string (e.g., ' Arrowhead Springs1231 '). Legacy combined identifier; preserved for audit trail but not used in canonical joins (prefer 3-tuple).

HorzPurchase: Date the lot was horizontally acquired by the developer — i.e., the land/finished-lot purchase by the horizontal developer entity (BCPD, ASD, BCPI, etc.). Stored as a US-format date string (e.g. '10/1/2024'). Source: Lot Data workbook. Earliest date in the per-lot lifecycle waterfall, preceding vertical purchase, start, CO, sale, and close.
HorzMDA: Master Development Agreement date (e.g., 10/1/2024). Administrative milestone; typically aligns with or follows HorzPurchase in the development timeline.
HorzPrelimPlat: Preliminary plat filing/approval date (e.g., 10/1/2024). Operational checkpoint in the horizontal phase; part of the lot-stage waterfall sequence.
HorzFinalPlat: Final plat recording date (e.g., 2/27/2025). Marks lot record completion; combined with HorzStart/HorzEnd to bracket the horizontal-development window.
HorzStart: Start of horizontal development work (e.g., 10/1/2024). Kickoff date for infrastructure, grading, utilities, and other phase-level costs; used to calculate current_stage via waterfall.
HorzEnd: End of horizontal development work (e.g., 6/30/2025). Marks completion of phase-level infrastructure; drives FINISHED_LOT state transition when combined with other date conditions.

HorzFinInv: Horizontal final invoice or completion-cost amount (float, nullable). Used to validate cost against GL cost accumulation; sparse fill suggests may be recalculated from GL rather than source-entered.
HorzRecord: Lot record/registration date (e.g., 2/27/2025). Typically aligns with HorzFinalPlat; marks the instant lot enters the lot-count universe for collateral and allocation purposes.
HorzWEnter: Water enter date or water-obligation start (float, nullable). Used in water-rights and water-obligation allocation; sparse fill indicates may be project-level rather than lot-specific.
HorzWExit: Water exit date or water-obligation end (float, nullable). Paired with HorzWEnter to bracket water-cost accrual window; sparse fill suggests project-level application.
HorzContract: Horizontal contract amount or contingency reserve (float, nullable). May represent builder agreement terms; sparse fill suggests used selectively or calculated downstream.

HorzSale: Lot sale date to vertical builder (e.g., 4/23/2025). Marks the buyer-acquisition moment; separated from VertPurchase to distinguish transaction timing. Nullable; some lots may be held unsold.

VertPurchase: Vertical builder lot-purchase date (e.g., 12/31/2025, 4/23/2025). Initiates vertical construction phase; typically same as or shortly after HorzSale. Excel sentinel 12/30/1899 treated as null (unoccurred).
VertStart: Vertical construction start date (e.g., 6/28/2025). Nullable; marks when home-building work begins, typically 2-6 months post-purchase. Used to drive VERTICAL_IN_PROGRESS state.

VertCO: Vertical certificate of occupancy / completion date (e.g., 10/15/2025, sentinel 12/30/1899=null). Marks home-build completion; drives VERTICAL_COMPLETE state. Excel sentinel 12/30/1899 is treated as unoccurred (lot not yet complete).

VertSale: Vertical home sale to end-buyer date (e.g., sentinel 12/30/1899=null). Marks listing/sale to retail buyer; Excel sentinel indicates lot not yet sold. Separate from VertClose (financing/closing).

VertClose: Vertical home closing/settlement date (e.g., sentinel 12/30/1899=null). Final transaction milestone; drives CLOSED state when ≤ as_of_date (2026-04-29). Excel sentinel 12/30/1899 indicates no closing yet.
Community name (e.g., Harmony, Parkway Fields) identifying the residential development project. Sourced from internal offer-pricing workbook tracking per-community, per-product-type cost structures.

Product Type: Product classification code (SFR=single-family residential, TH=townhome, MF=multifamily) indicating the unit type for which the cost structure applies. Used to segment pricing and cost models by development product mix.

Permit and fee cost in USD (formatted string with $ and decimals) for the specified community and product type. Represents regulatory and development-phase permit expenses; part of the per-unit horizontal cost model.

Base Costs: Base construction and development cost in USD (formatted string) per unit for the specified community and product type, excluding options and permits. Forms the core horizontal cost component in the offer-pricing and margin model.

Option Cost: Average optional upgrade or feature cost in USD (formatted string) per unit for the specified community and product type. Represents typical buyer-selected add-ons; used in margin and offer-price modeling.

Total: Sum of Permit + Base Costs + Option Cost in USD (formatted string), representing the per-unit total delivered cost for the specified community and product type. Primary input to offer-pricing and profitability analysis.
Unnamed: 0: Row index or sequence number; typically empty or used for row ordering in the source Excel export. Nullable float.
Unnamed: 1: Collateral asset class or pool label (e.g., 'Land + WIP + Homes Collateral'). Groups the subsequent rows by asset type for borrowing-base calculation.
Unnamed: 2: Collateral bucket or lifecycle stage within the asset pool (e.g., 'Sold, But Not Closed', 'Finished Homes', 'Model Homes'). Indicates lot status used to stratify advance rates.
Unnamed: 3: Intermediate calculation field; nullable float. Likely a quantity count or intermediate ratio used in the RBA (Receivables-Based Advance) / TNW (Total Net Worth) modeling.
Unnamed: 4: Intermediate calculation field; nullable float. Supports the borrowing-base computation chain (e.g., count × price or subtotal before advance-rate application).
Unnamed: 5: GAAP-basis collateral valuation (at cost). Numeric string with trailing spaces; values range from ~$3.4M to $64.5M. Represents accounting book value of the asset pool.

Unnamed: 6: Market-value collateral valuation (at appraised/market value). Numeric string with trailing spaces; values range from ~$3.8M to $89.2M. Typically higher than GAAP cost for appreciated inventory.
Unnamed: 7: Lender advance rate assumption per asset bucket (e.g., '90%', '80%'). Percentage string used to calculate potential borrowing base from collateral value.

Unnamed: 8: Potential Borrowing Base: the maximum lendable amount for each collateral bucket after applying the assumed advance rate. Computed as 'At Value' × 'Assumed Adv Rate'. Numeric string; ranges from ~$3.5M to $71.4M.

Line Type: Classifies posting rows as detail or summary entries. Samples show '  -- detail', indicating transaction-level GL postings (not rollup rows) within the Vertical Financials cost basis ledger.
Company: Numeric company code (1000 = Building Construction Partners, LLC / BCPD). Maps to canonical_entity for BCPD-scope filtering and multi-entity aggregation safety.

Company Name: Legal entity name ('Building Construction Partners, LLC'). Authoritative upstream source for canonical_entity mapping; used to distinguish BCPD from other entities (Hillcrest, Flagship Belmont, Lennar, external).
Division: Numeric division code (0 = Corporate). Operational segment within the company; almost always 0 in BCPD sample, indicating non-divisional posting.
Division Name: Textual division label ('Corporate'). Corresponds to Division code; used to segment cost by operational unit if non-zero divisions exist.

Account: Numeric legacy chart-of-accounts code (e.g., 1535 for Permits and Fees). VF uses a 4-digit asset-side account structure; safe to aggregate within VF/DR but never against QB (different chart).
Account Name: Textual account label (e.g., 'Permits and Fees'). Maps to canonical_cost_category (e.g., PERMITS_FEES) for cost-bucket rollups; identifies the business function of the posting.
Sub-Ledger: Subledger code (typically null in BCPD sample). Provides sub-account drill-down when populated; rarely used in this extract.
Sub-Ledger Name: Subledger label (typically null). Textual companion to Sub-Ledger code; populated only if sub-account tracking applies.
Account Type: Classification ('Asset'). Identifies GL role (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense). VF is asset-side debit-only; never appears as credit.
Account Group: Grouping category ('Balance Sheet'). Distinguishes balance-sheet accounts from P&L; used for reporting classification.
Manual RE Postings: Manual real-estate adjustment column (typically null). Allows manual corrections or write-backs to the posted amount; rarely used in BCPD sample.
Fiscal Year: Posting fiscal year (e.g., 2018). Used to filter time-window questions; VF sample spans 2018–2025.
First Day of FY: Fiscal year start date (typically null). Metadata field; not used for filtering in current pipeline.
Last Day of FY: Fiscal year end date (typically null). Metadata field; companion to Fiscal Year for period boundaries.
Opening Balance Date: Period opening balance timestamp (typically null). Reflects period-opening state; rarely populated in this extract.
Opening Balance: Opening balance dollar amount (typically null). Beginning balance for the period; not used for cost-basis rollups (debit-only feed).
Debit amount (typically null, rolled into Amount field). VF structure places the signed amount in Amount; Debit/Credit split is rarely populated.
Credit amount (typically null, rolled into Amount field). Companion to Debit; both null because Amount is the unified signed posting field.
Closing Balance: Closing balance (typically null). Period-end state; not used in cost-basis aggregation.
Closing Balance Date: Closing balance timestamp (typically null). Metadata; rarely used.
Posting Date: Transaction posting date (e.g., '6/26/2018'). Authoritative transaction date; used for fiscal-period bucketing and time-series filtering in cost rollups.
Posting Date FY: Fiscal year of Posting Date (e.g., 2018). Convenience field; redundant with Fiscal Year but ensures period alignment for cross-check.
Memo 1: Primary narrative field (e.g., 'Inv:Bld Permit HB29:Alpine City'). Encodes invoice/PO reference and vendor; parsed by dedup logic and stage pipeline for cost-category inference.
Memo 2: Secondary narrative field (typically null). Allows extended description; rarely used in BCPD sample.
Amount: Dollar amount as formatted string (e.g., '$16,508.59 '). Must be parsed to numeric; represents the signed posting amount (debit positive for asset accounts); core cost-basis field.
Str: Structure code ('H' = Harmony community). Two-letter project abbreviation; used alongside Project field to identify community; encodes project identity at source.
OUnit: Operating unit code (e.g., 'HB' = Harmony Build). Segment identifier within BCPD; used for operational cost tracking and routing within the company.
Project / community code in VF's own vocabulary (e.g., 'SctLot', 'Harm3', 'HarmCo'). Requires crosswalk to canonical_project; VF uses different code set than GL DR 38-col (eras pre/post-2018).
Lot number within the project (e.g., 29). Combined with Project to form (project, lot) pair for cost attribution; 100% populated on BCPD rows (primary authority for lot grain).
Major cost-category code (e.g., 1905, 1920). Internal VF cost rollup dimension; used alongside Minor for sub-account coding.
Minor cost-category code (typically 0 in sample). Sub-dimension of Major; allows granular cost segregation; nearly always 0 in BCPD rows.
Group OUnit: Group operating unit (e.g., 'HB'). Repeats OUnit; used for hierarchical cost aggregation.
Group No: Group identifier within the operating unit (e.g., 8084). Internal cost-tracking code; maps postings to cost pools or sub-projects within a phase.
Unit No: Unit number (e.g., 1, 48, 466). Further cost-tracking sub-dimension; distinguishes individual cost pools or cost-center line items.
Line No: Line sequence number within transaction (typically 1). Allows multi-line postings; almost always 1 (single-line transactions dominate).
Trans No: Transaction number (typically 0). Internal GL transaction ID; rarely used in BCPD extraction.
Journal Code: Journal type (e.g., 'PJ' = Purchase Journal). Indicates posting source system (PJ = PO/vendor invoice, AJ = Adjustment, etc.); used for audit trail and source-system classification.
Source system or matching method (e.g., 'PO Match'). Indicates whether posting came from PO, manual entry, or matched from vendor; used for data quality and audit.
Created By: User ID who created the posting (e.g., 'SusanP'). Audit trail; used to trace posting origin.
Create Date: Posting creation timestamp (e.g., '6/26/2018'). Distinct from Posting Date; reflects when the GL entry was created (may differ from transaction date).
Approved By: User ID who approved (e.g., 'terryw'). Workflow approval step; used for compliance and audit.
Approval Date: Date of approval (e.g., '6/26/2018'). May differ from Create Date; marks GL entry as finalized.
Approval Time: Time of approval as 24-hour integer (e.g., 1147 = 11:47 AM). Granular audit timestamp; rarely used operationally.
Refresh Date: Extract refresh timestamp (e.g., 'Feb 11 2026  1:12PM'). Metadata indicating when this VF CSV was exported; used to track data freshness (not Posting Date).
active: Active flag ('Y' = included, 'N' = archived). Filters live postings from inactive/reversed entries; always 'Y' in cost-basis rolls (inactive rows excluded at stage time).

datasources.description: Job Id: Optional external job/cost-center reference (float, nullable). Typically unpopulated in v3 snapshot; used for cross-reference to upstream accounting systems if needed.

datasources.description: Lot Type: Lot product type abbreviation (TH=Townhome, MF=Multifamily, SFR=Single-Family Residential, etc.). Segment used to differentiate allocation basis and margin assumptions within a phase.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Total Proj Sales: Total projected sales revenue for the phase × lot type cohort, formatted as currency string (e.g., '$0'). Denominator for sales-weighted allocation of indirect and land pools. Blank/zero indicates allocation basis incomplete or not yet projected.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Sales Basis % (Community): Community-level sales-basis weight as a percentage string (e.g., '0.0%'). Numerator for apportioning shared community indirect and land costs to this phase × lot type. Zero indicates no projected sales or allocation not yet modeled.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Community Indirect Pool: Total community-level indirect costs (engineering, planning, warranties, overhead) available for allocation, formatted as currency string (e.g., '$12,917,427'). Shared across all phases and lot types; distributed via Sales Basis % or lot-count weighting.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Indirect Allocated: Dollar amount of community indirect pool assigned to this phase × lot type, formatted as currency (e.g., '$0'). Calculated as Community Indirect Pool × Sales Basis %, or zero if sales/lot data insufficient. Feeds per-lot indirect cost basis.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Community Land Pool: Total community-level land/acquisition costs available for allocation, formatted as currency string (e.g., '$0'). Typically zero in v3 snapshot; when populated, distributed to phases via same sales or lot-count weighting as indirect pool.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Land Allocated: Dollar amount of community land pool assigned to this phase × lot type, formatted as currency (e.g., '$0'). Calculated pro-rata; zero in current v3 snapshot indicates no community-level land cost or no allocation basis yet defined.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Indirect & Land Pools
datasources.description: Type: Cost pool classification: either 'INDIRECT' (shared overhead routed across multiple phases/lots) or 'LAND' (land acquisition/carry costs). Sourced from Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 structure.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Indirect & Land Pools
datasources.description: Community: Canonical project / community name (e.g. 'Arrowhead Springs', 'Parkway Fields'). Joins to canonical_project for entity-level cost-pool aggregation.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Indirect & Land Pools
datasources.description: Job Id: Internal job identifier from the allocation workbook, linking cost pools to upstream project accounting codes or internal job-costing systems.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Indirect & Land Pools
datasources.description: Job Name: Human-readable cost-pool label (e.g. 'AS 1-3 Basin', 'AS 1-3 Storm Drain') describing the infrastructure or land component being budgeted.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Indirect & Land Pools
datasources.description: Current Budget (LandDev): Budgeted cost pool amount in USD (dollar-formatted string: '$486,638'). Represents the current land-development budget for this indirect or land cost bucket before any overrides.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Indirect & Land Pools
datasources.description: Budget Override: Optional numeric override (nullable float) allowing manual adjustment to the Current Budget. When populated, drives recomputation of Effective Budget; when null, Effective Budget equals Current Budget.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Indirect & Land Pools
datasources.description: Effective Budget: Final cost-pool budget in USD (dollar-formatted string) after applying any Budget Override. Used as the numerator for per-lot allocation when summed across all pools in a community phase.

datasources.field_name: Flagship Homes — Budget Allocation Workbook v2
sample_values: One row per phase per lot type (SFR, TH, MF, Condo).
LotData counts pre-populated. Enter manual lot counts (col F) and avg projected sales price (col H).
datasources.description: Flagship Homes — Budget Allocation Workbook v2: Multi-line instruction text describing the structure and usage of the Flagship Homes Budget Allocation Workbook v3. Contains tab-by-tab guidance on lot mix inputs, pricing, cost pools, and allocation methodology across the five-tab framework (Lot Mix & Pricing, Indirect & Land Pools, Engine, Per-Lot Output, and supporting reference tabs).

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Community / project name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs, Harmony). Joins to canonical_project in the allocation framework.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Phase / plat number within the community (e.g., 10, 11, 12). Joins to canonical_phase for cost allocation by phase and product type.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Job Id: Optional internal job or work-order identifier. Sparse; primarily null across the Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 framework.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Lot Type: Product type code (e.g., TH=townhome, MF=multifamily, or SFR=single-family). Used to disaggregate cost allocation and pricing assumptions by unit type within a phase.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Lots (LotData): Lot count per phase and product type, sourced from Lot Data CSV. Used as the primary denominator for per-lot cost allocation when Manual Input is unpopulated.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Lots (Manual Input): Override lot count if the workbook user needs to adjust from Lot Data (e.g., to reflect planned/future lots). Sparse; when populated, feeds Tie-Out calculation.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Tie-Out: Lot count validation field; equals Manual Input if populated, otherwise equals Lots (LotData). Ensures the allocation denominator is explicit and traceable.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Avg Projected Sales Price: Average selling price per lot for the phase and product type. Sparse across v3 framework; required to compute total projected revenue and margin.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Lot Mix & Pricing
datasources.description: Total Projected Sales: Total projected revenue (lot count × average price) for the phase and product type. Displayed as a formatted currency string (e.g., '$0'). Currently showing $0 across sampled rows, indicating the workbook cells are unpopulated for most BCPD projects beyond Lomond Heights and Parkway Fields.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Community: Canonical project/community name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs, Harmony). Links rows to the phase-level allocation framework defined elsewhere in the Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Phase or plat number within the community (e.g., 10, 11, 12). Combined with Community, identifies the phase-level budget pool from which direct/indirect/land costs are allocated.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Job Id: Optional internal job or lot identifier; sparse/unpopulated in current snapshot. Nullable float field; rarely filled.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Lot Type: Product type classification (TH=townhome, MF=multifamily, or SF=single-family). Drives which cost pool and allocation method applies to this lot row.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Lots: Count of individual lots represented by this row; typically 1 for per-lot rows, or >1 for aggregate rows. Nullable; mostly unpopulated in current snapshot.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Direct Budget (LandDev): Planned direct horizontal/land-development cost sourced from the LandDev pool; formatted as currency string (e.g., '$0', '$125,000'). Empty cells ('$0') indicate cells not yet populated in v3 framework.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Direct Budget Override: Manual override amount for direct cost, if any. Nullable float; allows local adjustments to the LandDev-derived budget. When populated, overrides the LandDev value.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Effective Direct Budget: Reconciled direct cost per lot; equals Override if populated, else falls back to Direct Budget (LandDev). Currency string ($). Controls the direct-cost numerator in per-lot rollups.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Indirect Allocated: Indirect cost allocation (e.g., shared infrastructure, admin, utilities) assigned to this lot from the phase-level indirect pool. Currency string ($); mostly '$0' in current v3 snapshot.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Land Allocated: Land acquisition cost allocated to this lot. Currency string ($); reflects the phase's total land cost divided across the lot count. Mostly '$0' in current snapshot.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Total Cost: Sum of Effective Direct Budget + Indirect Allocated + Land Allocated. Expected total cost per lot. Currency string ($); row-level outcome metric.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Direct / Lot: Unit cost: Effective Direct Budget ÷ Lots. Currency string ($); enables per-lot granularity when Lots > 1. Mostly '$0' in current snapshot.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Indirect / Lot: Unit cost: Indirect Allocated ÷ Lots. Currency string ($); per-lot indirect component for margin analysis. Mostly '$0' in current snapshot.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Land / Lot: Unit cost: Land Allocated ÷ Lots. Currency string ($); per-lot land cost component. Mostly '$0' in current snapshot.

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Per-Lot Output
datasources.description: Total / Lot: Unit cost: Total Cost ÷ Lots. Final per-lot cost basis for margin, pricing, and carry-cost calculations. Currency string ($); primary output for cost accounting.
Unnamed: 0: Week identifier or row label (e.g., 'Wk 1') for the planned-starts forecast table. Purpose unclear from sample alone; appears to index forecast rows.

Planned Starts 2021- 13/ week : Header row carrying month names (January, February, etc.) and plan codes (H121, SL 2603). Semantics mixed; appears to conflate calendar months with product-type or phase identifiers. Likely a malformed or transposed header from the source workbook.

Unnamed: 2: Plan name or lot identifier for February column. Contains values like 'SL 2603', 'SL 2604' (Scattered Lots plan codes per v0 decoder). Semantics: planned lot starts in February; product or plan type unknown from sample.
Unnamed: 3: Plan name or lot identifier for March column. Contains 'H134', 'H129' (Harmony phase/lot prefixes). Planned lot starts in March.
Unnamed: 4: Plan name or lot identifier for April column. Contains 'SL 25 C' (Scattered Lots plan variant). Planned lot starts in April.
Unnamed: 5: Plan name or lot identifier for May column. Contains 'SL 24T' (Scattered Lots plan variant). Planned lot starts in May.
Unnamed: 6: Plan name or lot identifier for June column. Contains 'SL 24T' (Scattered Lots plan variant). Planned lot starts in June.
Unnamed: 7: Plan name or lot identifier for July column. Contains 'SL 24T' (Scattered Lots plan variant). Planned lot starts in July.

ACTUAL: Temporal marker for the closing cohort; values like 'January' or numeric day indicators identify which month or reporting period within 2018 the closing occurred. Used to organize and filter historical closing records by time bucket.

SUBDIV: Community / subdivision abbreviation (e.g., 'SL' for Scattered Lots, 'SPRINGS' for Arrowhead Springs). Maps to canonical_project via the SUBDIV_TO_PROJECT crosswalk; whitespace-normalized at stage time.

PH: Phase / plat identifier within the subdivision (e.g., '15', '4B'). Normalized whitespace per v1 pipeline; joins with canonical_phase on (canonical_project, canonical_phase) tuple. GL data carries 0% phase fill; phase originates from operational sources like this.

LOT #: Lot number as a numeric or numeric-prefix identifier (e.g., 1520.0, 87.0). Normalized to canonical_lot_number (strip .0, strip leading zeros); joins on 3-tuple (canonical_project, canonical_phase, canonical_lot_number) to prevent Harmony-style MF1/B1 lot 101–116 collisions.

BUYER                              : End buyer name (individual or entity); natural-language free text (e.g., 'Tanna and Terry McIntosh'). Used for audit trail and buyer-linked reporting; not a cost or operational state field.

SALE DATE: Date the lot was sold to the buyer (contract signing). Timestamp format; used to derive lot_status (CLOSED if sale_date ≤ as_of_date) and to calculate DAYS SINCE SALE. Excel sentinel 1899-12-30 treated as null.

DAYS SINCE SALE: Calculated field: days elapsed from SALE DATE to report run date (volatile; dropped at stage time). Useful for operational velocity tracking but not suitable for canonical state since it changes every day.

Permit Pulled: Date when building permit was issued for the lot. Sourced from the Inventory _ Closing Report workbook (2018 Closings sheet); used to track horizontal development progress and timing of vertical construction commencement.

C of O: Certificate of Occupancy (CO) date for the structure. Timestamp; represents vertical construction completion (VERTICAL_COMPLETE state in waterfall). Distinct from CLOSED DATE (financial closing) and SALE DATE (contract).

CLOSED DATE: Date the financial closing occurred (escrow closes, title transfers). Timestamp; the authoritative lot_status='CLOSED' trigger. Staged as closing_date_actual in inventory snapshot when closing_date ≤ as_of_date.

datasources.field_name: Permit Pull              to C of O
datasources.description: Permit Pull              to C of O: Calculated duration in days from permit issuance to certificate of occupancy. Metric for construction cycle velocity; dropped at stage time (volatile, not canonical state).

Average Construction Days: Phase-level or community-level aggregate: mean days from permit pull to CO across all lots in the cohort. Reporting metric; not lot-grain. Not carried into per-lot canonical state.

Projected Margin: Expected gross margin percentage (0–1 scale) on lot sale, calculated as (sales_price − total_cost_basis) / sales_price. Reflects anticipated profitability at time of projection; subject to revision as actual costs accrue or sales price changes.

ACTUAL: Month or numeric identifier for actual closings completed in 2020. Mixed data type (text month names like 'January' and numeric values like 21) suggests column carries both period labels and closing counts or IDs.
SUBDIV: Community or subdivision name where the lot is located (e.g., WINDSOR, SL for Scattered Lots). Links to canonical_project via the SUBDIV_TO_PROJECT mapping in the inventory staging pipeline.
LOT #: Lot number within the subdivision. Numeric identifier (37, 32, 1807) normalized to canonical_lot_number during staging; joins with phase and project to form canonical_lot_id.

BUYER                              : Name(s) of the buyer(s) or purchaser(s) at closing. Free-text field; carries institutional knowledge about who acquired each lot but is not used in cost allocation or financial rollups.

SALE DATE: Date the lot was sold or offer accepted (e.g., 2019-11-13). Used to derive lot_status (ACTIVE if sale_date is populated but closing_date > as_of_date) and to calculate days-to-close duration metrics.

DAYS SINCE SALE: Calculated field: days elapsed from SALE DATE to the as_of_date or report generation date. Values like 2339, 2306 suggest this is a snapshot of historical days; not used in current v2.1 canonical state (volatile). Dropped at stage time.

Permit Pulled: Date when the building permit was issued for the lot. Sourced from the Inventory Closing Report workbook's historical 2020 Closings sheet; populated for lots that progressed through permitting in 2019–2020.

C of O: Certificate of Occupancy (C of O) issuance date, marking vertical construction completion. Sourced from the Inventory Closing Report workbook; typically follows Permit Pulled by 6–12 months and precedes final closing.

CLOSED DATE: Closing (escrow) date when title transferred and transaction settled (e.g., 2020-01-08). Used to classify lot_status: CLOSED if closing_date ≤ as_of_date, ACTIVE_PROJECTED if closing_date > as_of_date. Authoritative for final closing timing.

datasources.field_name: Permit Pull              to C of O
datasources.description: Permit Pull              to C of O: Duration in days from permit issuance to certificate of occupancy (e.g., 173, 124 days). Derived metric for construction cycle length; useful for operational timeline visibility but not used in financial allocations.

Average Construction Days: Aggregate metric: mean construction duration (e.g., 180.47 days) across multiple lots or a period. Summary statistic, not lot-grain; dropped at stage and reconstructed from individual lot timelines if needed.

Projected Margin: Expected margin or profit per lot (empty in samples). Field structure reserved for margin calculation but unpopulated in this snapshot; would be derived from sales_price minus lot_cost_basis if populated.
: Project or community identifier (e.g., 'SL' for Scattered Lots, 'HARMONY'). Denotes the development community that contains the lot. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
PH: Phase number within the project, stored as numeric value (e.g., 8.0, 10.0). Identifies the plat or development phase. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
LOT #: Lot number within the phase, stored as numeric value (e.g., 802.0, 1028.0). Uniquely identifies the individual buildable parcel. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
PLAN: Plan or model name for the lot (e.g., 'Redwood A', 'Spruce B'). Identifies the home design type assigned to or sold for this lot. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
BUYER: Name of the buyer or purchasing entity (e.g., 'MODEL-Renting', 'Tyler and Rachel Herbert'). May be null for unsold lots. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
SALES PRICE: Sale price in USD (e.g., $521,000, $501,371). May be null for unsold or model lots. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
DEPOSITS: Buyer deposit amount in USD (e.g., $5,000, $4,000). Earnest money or down payment. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.

SALE DATE: Date when the lot was sold or purchase agreement executed (timestamp). Used to derive current lot_status (ACTIVE/CLOSED) and as_of_date anchor. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.

DAYS SINCE SALE/SPEC START: Elapsed days from sale date (or spec start for model homes) as of the inventory snapshot. Calculated field; indicates active-pipeline age. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.

PERMIT PULLED
: Date when the building permit was pulled/issued (timestamp). Marks transition to vertical construction phase. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
Permit number: Municipal permit identifier (string, e.g., '2016-1071', 'R-24-255'). Links to permitting authority records. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
Margin: Profit margin as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.1883 = 18.83%). Calculated as (Sales Price − Total Cost) / Sales Price. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.

Unnamed: 12: Unnamed/unlabeled numeric field (float, sample value 0.153). Semantic meaning unclear from available metadata; likely a duplicate or trailing margin-related calculation. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report INVENTORY sheet.
: Community or subdivision name, serving as the primary lot-location identifier within the Inventory Closing Report's CLOSINGS sheet (forecast/pending-close tab).
Unnamed: 1: Lot number within the community/subdivision, identifying the specific buildable parcel associated with the pending or closed sale transaction.
Unnamed: 2: Buyer name(s) on the purchase agreement or closing document, identifying the purchaser(s) of the lot.
Unnamed: 3: Ratified sales date (contract execution or offer acceptance date), marking when the purchase agreement became binding. Format: ISO 8601 timestamp.
CLOSED=: Count of days elapsed since the ratified sales date, computed at report generation time. Provides aging visibility on how long a sale has been pending closing.

Scheduled at Title: Estimated closing date projected by title company or lender, indicating when the transaction is expected to fund and record. Used to generate `closing_date_projected` in canonical inventory state (where actual closing_date ≤ as_of_date is unavailable).
Unnamed: 6: Funded flag (marked 'x' if true), indicating whether the loan has been funded and the transaction is ready to record at the county recorder.
Unnamed: 7: Final Addendum flag (marked 'x' if executed), signaling whether all closing addenda (price adjustments, repair credits, HOA docs) have been fully executed by both parties.
WATCHING=: Free-text notes field capturing closing status updates, extension reasons, contingency notes, or other material events affecting the transaction timeline.

C of O=: Certificate of Occupancy date, marking when the vertical construction was substantially complete and the unit became legally occupiable. Maps to `actual_c_of_o` in ClickUp and operational state.
Unnamed: 10: Lender identity (e.g. 'Outside Lender' or specific institution name), indicating the source of financing for the buyer's mortgage.
Unnamed: 11: Flagship Homes realtor/sales agent name assigned to the transaction, representing Flagship's interest in the sale.
Unnamed: 12: Outside realtor name (buyer's agent or co-listing agent if transaction involved external representation), supporting dual-agent closing workflows.
Unnamed: 13: Outside realtor phone number, providing contact information for the external real-estate agent involved in the transaction.
Unnamed: 14: Referral fee field (column header present; data values sparse), intended to capture compensation owed to external agents or referral sources per transaction.
: Subdivision code identifying the residential community or project (e.g., LEC for Lomond Heights East). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet, row-header field.
Unnamed: 1: Phase identifier within the subdivision, typically numeric (1, 2, etc.) or alphanumeric (A, B1). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; used to partition lots within a community.
Unnamed: 2: Lot number within the phase, typically numeric. Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; primary key component for lot identity alongside subdivision and phase.
Unnamed: 3: Home plan or model name (e.g., Harvard, Brentwood). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; identifies the architectural plan / product type of the built home.
Unnamed: 4: Closing date of the home sale transaction. Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; marks the settlement date when title transferred and sale was recorded. Null when not yet closed.
Unnamed: 5: Buyer name(s) for the closed sale. Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; identifies the purchaser(s) of the lot and home.

Flagship Homes Current Inventory Report: Sale price of the home (numeric amount in USD). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; contract sales price agreed at offer, sometimes formatted as timestamp in raw export but represents a dollar figure.

Unnamed: 7: Deposit amount received from buyer, typically marked 'X' if collected or numeric if amount tracked. Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; earnest money or initial deposit toward purchase.
Unnamed: 8: Sale date (date the offer was accepted / contract signed). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; distinct from closing_date; marks the contract-formation event.

Unnamed: 9: Days elapsed since the sale date (SALE DATE column), calculated as a derived field at time of report generation. Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; volatile formula-based field; dropped at stage time in v2.1.
Unnamed: 10: Permit pulled date (when building permit was issued by jurisdiction). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; marks the start of the construction phase for the vertical builder.
Unnamed: 11: Dig date (first shovel / groundbreaking / excavation date for site preparation). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; marks the start of horizontal development work on the lot.
Unnamed: 12: Anticipated or scheduled completion date for the home construction. Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; forward-looking; may differ from actual completion if schedule slips.

Unnamed: 13: Permit number assigned by the jurisdiction (building permit ID). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; references the official jurisdiction permit document; sometimes appears as a date in raw export due to column merging.

Unnamed: 14: Water rights landscape check received flag (typically 'Yes', 'No', or blank). Source: Inventory Closing Report CLOSED sheet; confirms water-rights documentation and landscape compliance milestone.
ACTUAL: Month of actual closing, text label (e.g. 'January'). Categorizes rows by which month the transaction closed within the current year.

SUBDIV: Subdivision / community code, normalized abbreviation (e.g. 'SL' = Scattered Lots, 'MEADOW CREEK'). Used to identify the project and cross-reference to canonical_project via the subdivision crosswalk.
LOT #: Lot number within the subdivision, numeric identifier. Joins to canonical_lot_number after normalization (e.g. strip leading zeros). Primary key component alongside SUBDIV for lot identity.
BUYER                              : Purchaser name, free-text. Records the buyer(s) on the closed transaction; used for audit and contact tracking but not operational cost allocation.

SALE DATE: Date the lot was sold to the buyer. Used to compute lot lifecycle stage (SOLD_NOT_CLOSED if closing_date > as_of) and to flag rows in the CLOSED tab as forward-projected (closing_date > as_of_date = 2026-04-29).
DAYS SINCE SALE: Calendar days elapsed from SALE DATE to as_of_date or current observation date. Derived metric; deprecated at stage time in favor of date-arithmetic queries.

Permit Pulled/Dig Date: Date the building permit was issued or the lot was first dug. Marks the start of horizontal development activity. Used to derive current_stage = HORIZONTAL_IN_PROGRESS when this date is populated and ≤ as_of_date.

C of O: Certificate of Occupancy date (vertical completion). Marks the transition from VERTICAL_IN_PROGRESS to VERTICAL_COMPLETE in the lot-state waterfall. Critical lifecycle date for cost recognition.

CLOSED DATE: Final closing / title transfer date. Defines lot_status = CLOSED (if closing_date ≤ as_of_date 2026-04-29) or ACTIVE_PROJECTED (if closing_date > as_of). Authoritative endpoint for per-lot financial settlement.

datasources.field_name: Permit Pull              to C of O
datasources.description: Permit Pull              to C of O: Days from Permit Pulled/Dig Date to C of O; construction duration. Operational metric used to assess phase-level construction pace and identify schedule variances.
Average Construction Days: Phase-level average of construction days (Permit Pull to C of O) for all lots in the phase. Summary statistic; appears once per phase grouping, null for individual lots.

Projected Margin: Estimated or realized margin (sales_price - total_cost) as a dollar amount. Null in sampled rows; expected to populate for closed transactions. Used for post-close profitability analysis.
Unnamed: 12: Unlabeled numeric column (empty in samples). Likely a residual Excel column with no semantic meaning; recommend inspection of workbook column headers to confirm intent.

Unnamed: 13: Unlabeled text column; sample value 'Projected to close'. Appears to flag forward-projected closings from the CLOSED tab (future-dated rows). Acts as a status marker for non-yet-closed transactions.

Unnamed: 14: Unlabeled text column; sample values 'PARKWAY'. Redundant copy of project / subdivision name, likely from a second data section or tab within the 2025 Closings workbook. Recommend deduplication during intake.

Unnamed: 15: Unlabeled text column; sample values '299B', '332', '297B'. Appears to be a lot identifier or phase suffix; likely a duplicate or variant lot numbering scheme. Clarify relationship to LOT # field.
Unnamed: 16: Unlabeled text column; buyer names (e.g. 'Brooke and Wade Roundy'). Duplicate of the named BUYER column, likely from a second data region. Recommend deduplication.

Unnamed: 17: Unlabeled timestamp column; sample dates 2025-03-01 onwards. Appears to be a forward-projected or planned closing date for the ACTIVE_PROJECTED tab rows. Use to derive closing_date_projected when > as_of_date.

Unnamed: 18: Unlabeled numeric column; sample values 404.0, 393.0, 389.0. Likely a days-elapsed metric (e.g. DAYS SINCE SALE or similar projection). Inspect workbook to confirm calculation and intent.
Unnamed: 19: Unlabeled numeric column (empty in samples). Likely a reserved or placeholder field in the original workbook. Recommend removal unless workbook structure indicates otherwise.
Unnamed: 20: Unlabeled numeric column (empty in samples). Same as Unnamed: 19 — likely reserved. Recommend removal.

Unnamed: 21: Unlabeled timestamp column; sample dates 2025-04-16. Likely an as_of_date or data-refresh-date field marking when the projection was generated. Not a lot-specific date; recommend moving to metadata.

ACTUAL: Month or numeric identifier for the closing transaction. Mixes text months (e.g. 'January') with numeric values (e.g. 17), suggesting either a parsed month name or day-of-month in a summary row.

SUBDIV: Community/subdivision code (e.g. 'HARMONY', 'HARMONY TH'). Maps to canonical_project via subdivision crosswalk; 'HARMONY TH' denotes townhome product type within Harmony. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report workbook.

: Timestamp field (likely a date column header or index); contains 2025-01-01 as sample. Purpose unclear from the metadata; may represent report generation date or data snapshot date.
Phase identifier for the lot within the community, stored as float (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Nullable; sample shows empty values, suggesting this field is sparsely populated or used only for certain records.

Sales Price: Actual or contracted sales price in USD (e.g., $495,420, $478,274). The revenue side of the margin calculation; reflects the buyer's offer or closing price.
Margin: Gross margin in USD (e.g., $87,564, $100,693). Calculated as Sales Price minus the lot's total development cost (Permits + Direct Construction + allocated shared costs); before commissions.
Margin %: Gross margin as a percentage of sales price (e.g., 17.67%, 21.05%, 22.36%). Normalized measure of profitability per lot; = Margin / Sales Price.
Outside Commission: Third-party sales commission or realtor fee in USD (e.g., $3,400, $14,359.86). Deducted from gross margin to compute final margin; represents external sales expenses.
Final Margin: Net margin after outside commissions in USD (e.g., $84,164, $97,293, $94,816.14). = Margin - Outside Commission; the true profit to BCPD on this lot.
Final Margin %: Final margin as a percentage of sales price (e.g., 16.99%, 20.34%, 19.42%). Normalized post-commission profitability; = Final Margin / Sales Price.
Estimated Margin: Forecast or budget margin in USD (e.g., $88,557, $96,426, $83,249). Projection from allocation / underwriting model; compared against actual margins for variance analysis.
Estimated Margin %: Forecast margin as a percentage of sales price (e.g., 17.88%, 20.16%, 17.05%). Budgeted profitability target from allocation workbook.
Final vs Estimated: Margin variance in USD (e.g., -$4,393, +$867, +$11,567.14). = Final Margin - Estimated Margin; positive = beat forecast, negative = underperformed forecast.
Final vs. Estimated: Margin variance as a percentage of estimated margin (e.g., -0.89%, +0.18%, +2.37%). Normalized variance metric; signals relative performance against forecast.

Unnamed: 85: Row-level metric category identifier holding values 'Sales', 'Starts', or 'Closings' to indicate which operational metric (lot closings, construction starts, sales activity) the forecasted/actual columns above measure.
SUPERVISOR: Superintendent or project manager name responsible for overseeing the construction of the lot. Single-value field from the operational context, not row-specific to individual lots.

Average Construction Days: Mean duration in calendar days from permit issuance to certificate of occupancy across lots supervised by the named superintendent. Derived metric used to benchmark construction velocity and supervisor performance.

Subdivision : Phase or plat subdivision identifier, sometimes including a total aggregate row. Denormalized label; values like 'Total ' and 'SL S' / 'SL T' suggest phase-letter suffixes (e.g. Salem Fields S track vs T track).
SUBDIV: Canonical subdivision / project abbreviation (e.g. 'SL' for Salem Fields). Used with PH to form the (project, phase) key for operational grouping.
PH: Phase number within the subdivision (e.g. 15 for Salem Fields Phase 15). Integer; combined with SUBDIV to identify the phase; maps to canonical_phase via crosswalk.
LOT #: Lot number within the phase. Integer; combined with SUBDIV and PH to form the full lot identifier; maps to canonical_lot_number via normalization.

Permit Pulled: Date the building permit was issued to the vertical builder. ISO 8601 timestamp; marks the start of the construction-duration window used to compute Average Construction Days and days-to-C/O metrics.

C of O: Certificate of occupancy (C/O) issuance date for the lot. ISO 8601 timestamp; marks the end of the construction-duration window and the point at which the lot transitions to ready-to-close state.

CLOSED DATE: Final closing (escrow settlement) date for the lot sale. ISO 8601 timestamp; typically follows C/O by a small number of days; used to mark the lot's progression to CLOSED lifecycle state.

datasources.field_name: Permit Pull              to C of O
datasources.description: Permit Pull              to C of O: Calculated duration in calendar days between Permit Pulled and C of O dates. Integer; directly comparable to Average Construction Days; used to measure individual-lot construction velocity against supervisor and phase benchmarks.
: Project or phase identifier; contains values like 'June 2017 Starts' and 'SL' (likely Scattered Lots or similar). Marks the operational grouping for lots tracked in this 2018 milestone snapshot.

Unnamed: 1: Numeric phase or section code; sample values 15.0 appear to be phase identifiers or lot-range starts used in conjunction with the first column to locate specific lots within the tracking cohort.
Unnamed: 2: Numeric lot identifier or lot-number component; values 1505.0, 1508.0 represent individual lot numbers within the phase, keyed to the phase code in the prior column.

Unnamed: 3: Milestone event label and/or sale date; contains headers ('STARTS', 'Date of Sale') and ISO datetime strings (e.g. '2017-06-16T00:00:00'). Represents the date a lot was sold or a construction phase commenced.

Unnamed: 4: Permit event label and/or permit-pulled date; contains 'Permit Pulled' header and ISO datetime values (e.g. '2017-06-14T00:00:00'). Tracks when construction permits were issued for lots in this cohort.

>42 days: Days-elapsed metric or threshold label; mixes descriptive text ('>56 days', 'Days Since Sale') with numeric values (-2, etc.), measuring the time window between sale and subsequent operational milestone (permit, dig, or C of O).
Unnamed: 6: Summary statistic label; contains 'Average', suggesting this column rolls up a days-elapsed metric across lots in the cohort (e.g., average days from sale to permit).

Unnamed: 7: Construction start event label and/or dig date; contains 'CONSTRUCTION', 'Starts (Digs)', and ISO datetime strings (e.g. '2017-07-13T00:00:00'). Marks when ground-breaking or foundation work commenced.
Unnamed: 8: Days-elapsed from permit to dig; contains header 'Days Since Permit' and numeric values (29, 57), measuring the cycle time between permit issuance and construction start.

Unnamed: 9: Certificate of Occupancy (C of O) estimate label and/or date; contains 'Estimated C of O' header and ISO datetime values (e.g. '2017-12-10T00:00:00'). Forecasts when the lot/home will be ready for occupancy.
Unnamed: 10: Actual Certificate of Occupancy date; contains 'C of O' header and ISO datetime strings (e.g. '2018-01-24T00:00:00'). Records when the home actually achieved occupancy readiness.
Unnamed: 11: Construction duration label; contains 'Dig To C of O', labeling the elapsed time span from ground-breaking to final occupancy approval.

sample_values: Days past Est. C of O
datasources.description: Unnamed: 12: Days variance from C of O estimate; contains 'Days past Est. C of O' header and numeric values (45, 38), measuring schedule slippage (actual vs. forecasted occupancy date).

>160 days: Final milestone duration label and/or threshold; contains '>180 days', 'Sale to CO', and numeric values (222), summing the total days from sale through certificate of occupancy to assess project cycle time.
Months: Calendar month name (August, September, October) serving as a time-period label for this operational metrics row.
Start to Permit : Count of calendar days elapsed between lot horizontal start date and permit pulled date, measured in days. Sample range 49–68 days indicates typical permitting cycle duration.
Month: Duplicate calendar month name field; appears to label the same time period as the first Months column.
Permit to Dig : Count of calendar days elapsed between permit pulled date and dig/grading start date, measured in days. Sample range 12–23 days indicates typical pre-construction lag.
Month.1: Second duplicate calendar month name field; consistent with prior month columns, likely a data-entry or pivot artifact.

Construction Days: Count of calendar days required to complete vertical construction (from dig start through certificate of occupancy), measured in days. Sample range 182–220 days reflects typical single-family construction duration.
Month.2: Third duplicate calendar month name field; echoes the time-period label across all metric sections.
# of Permits Pulled: Count of residential permits issued during the named month, aggregated across active projects. Sample range 19–39 permits reflects monthly permitting volume.
2019 Forecast : Row header identifying the forecast category (SPRINGS community) and metric type (Sales or Starts) for this row. Text values anchor the multi-level month/quarter/period structure.

GOAL : Monthly closing target or forecast identifier (e.g., 'January', 'February'). Appears to group actual closings by intended month.
ACTUAL: Count of lots actually closed in the period. Numeric values (2.0, 6.0) indicate volume of closings achieved against the GOAL target.
SUBDIV: Subdivision or project code abbreviation (LC=Lomond Creek, SL=Scattered Lots, SCR=Scarlet Ridge, etc.). Identifies which Flagship community the lot belongs to.
PH: Phase identifier within the subdivision, mixed format (numeric '4', '10' or alpha 'K'). Combined with SUBDIV to locate the lot within a community's development phases.
LOT #: Lot number within the phase (121, 1042, 7, etc.). Primary business identifier for the individual buildable parcel; combined with PH and SUBDIV forms the lot address.
BUYER                              : Homebuyer name(s) at closing. Personal identifier of the end purchaser (e.g., 'Alec and Courtney Salmon'). Used for closing records and buyer correspondence.
SALE DATE: Date the lot was sold / purchase agreement signed (2015-08-20, 2015-08-27, etc.). Marks the beginning of the vertical construction and closing workflow for this lot.
DAYS SINCE SALE: Elapsed days from SALE DATE to the as-of date of this report snapshot (3,885, 3,878, 3,744 days). Operational metric for tracking sale-to-close cycle duration.
START DATE: Date vertical construction began on the lot (2015-10-01, 2015-09-17, etc.). Marks the transition from land development to home building phase.

datasources.description:
EST.
C of O 
ON / BEFORE: Estimated or planned certificate-of-occupancy date, the target completion milestone (2016-01-06, 2016-01-12, etc.). Forward-looking commitment for buyer and construction scheduling.
CLOSED DATE: Actual closing date when title transferred and funds settled (2016-01-06, 2016-01-13, 2016-02-03, etc.). Final lifecycle event; marks lot as CLOSED in inventory status.
Unnamed: 11: Days value (97, 117, 202) with no header label in source. Likely construction cycle duration or similar derived field; semantics unclear from available context.
Unnamed: 12: Numeric value (107, 149.5) with no header label. Appears sparse (not all rows populated); purpose cannot be inferred from field name or samples alone.
Sales Price: Final sales price paid by buyer at closing (314,600.0 shown). Revenue recognition field for margin and profitability calculations at lot grain.
Margin: Realized profit in dollars per lot (57,932, 36,385, 51,398, etc.). Computed as Sales Price minus total development cost basis allocated to the lot.
Margin %: Margin as a percentage of sales price (0.1633757152 = ~16.3%). Profitability metric; enables cross-lot and cross-phase margin comparisons.

: Month label header; carries period identifier (e.g., 'January ') for the closing-date grouping within this sheet. Operational metadata only.
ACTUAL: Count of closings within the month. Numeric tally (e.g., 11 closings in January) tracking transaction volume by period.
SUBDIV: Subdivision / community code abbreviation (e.g., 'SL' for Scattered Lots, 'CASCADE' for Cascade). Maps to canonical_project via subdivision crosswalk. Source: Inventory _ Closing Report.
PH: Phase identifier within the subdivision (e.g., '12', '5A'). Whitespace-normalized per canonical_phase. Operational phase label from inventory source.
LOT #: Lot number within the phase (e.g., 1204, 87). Numeric identifier; normalizes to canonical_lot_number (strip .0, preserve alpha suffix). Source: Inventory _ Closing Report.
BUYER                              : Name(s) of the closing buyer(s) (e.g., 'Douglas and Andrea Filipe'). Identifies the end purchaser at closing; used for transaction audit and customer records.
SALE DATE: Date the lot was sold (offer accepted). Timestamp (e.g., 2016-05-21). Marks the start of the sales-to-close timeline; distinguishes from permit_pulled_date and closing_date.
DAYS SINCE SALE: Calculated days elapsed from SALE DATE to the sheet's reference date (likely report-run date ~2026). Operational aging metric; derived as a delta, not a master-data field.

Permit Pulled: Date building permit was issued / pulled by the builder. Timestamp (e.g., 2016-07-13). Marks vertical construction start; aligns with lot_status transitions and VERTICAL_IN_PROGRESS stage entry.
C of O: Certificate of Occupancy date; marks completion of vertical construction and occupancy readiness. Timestamp (e.g., 2017-01-04). Maps to current_stage VERTICAL_COMPLETE or synonym.

CLOSED DATE: Date closing was recorded / funds transferred; final lot transaction. Timestamp (e.g., 2017-01-04). Authoritative lot_status marker: if closing_date ≤ as_of_date → CLOSED, else → ACTIVE or ACTIVE_PROJECTED.
