Project: Community / development project name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs, Harmony). Links to canonical_project via 2025Status source data; authoritative for project identity per source_to_field_map.md.

Phase: Numeric phase or stage identifier from the 2025Status internal workbook. Sample values (123) appear to be encoded phase codes rather than textual phase names; context suggests these may represent phase numbers or internal classification codes used in the collateral/status tracking system.
Lot number within the phase (integer 1, 2, 3, etc.). Normalizes to canonical_lot_number; combined with Project and Phase forms the 3-tuple join key for VF cost matching (Guardrail 4).
Product Type: Residential product category: SFR (single-family), MF (multifamily), or other. Distinguishes lot type for cost allocation and buyer profile analysis.
Lot Count: Count of lots in this row's aggregation level (typically 1 for detail rows). Supports rollup validation; sum across a phase gives phase lot count for denominator checks.

Status: Per-lot lifecycle stage in the 2025 status workbook, encoded as a numeric code plus label (e.g. '13 - Vert Purchase', '15 - Vert CO'). Captures where the lot currently sits in the horizontal/vertical build and sale pipeline; pairs with Status Date for the timestamp of the latest transition. Source: 2025Status workbook.
Status Date: Date of the most recent status transition (e.g., '12/31/2025', '10/15/2025'). Supports timeline analysis and distinguishes forecast-dated (future) from actual-dated (≤as_of_date) states.
Vert Sold: Boolean indicator ('Yes' / 'No') of vertical sale occurrence. Corroborates vertical-sale stage; complements Lot Data lifecycle dates for stage derivation.
Collateral Bucket: Lender collateral classification (e.g., '3 - Finished Lots', '5 - Complete & Unsold'). Maps status to advance-rate buckets; drives borrowing-base weighting per collateral_snapshot.

Permits and Fees: Horizontal development cost for permits and regulatory fees (string form with spaces, e.g., '  47,037.08 '). Numeric substring extracts to actual_cost via cost_category='PERMITS_FEES'; part of per-lot horizontal cost basis.

Direct Construction - Lot: Direct lot-specific construction cost (string form, e.g., '  90,801.95 ' or '  -   ' for null). Extracts to actual_cost via cost_category='DIRECT_CONSTRUCTION_LOT'; distinguishes lot-direct from shared/indirect.
Direct Construction: Total direct construction cost across lot + shared (string form). Denominator for allocation pool; sum used in phase-level cost rollups from 2025Status.

Vertical Costs: Vertical (builder) costs incurred per lot (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$226,478'). Denotes phase 2 (vertical) spend; supplements GL VF costs where 2025Status provides detail GL lacks.

Shared Cost Alloc.: Allocated portion of shared infrastructure costs per lot (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$31'). Part of phase-level allocation model; rolls to lot_cost when combined with direct lot costs.

Lot Cost: Total per-lot horizontal cost (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$0', '$90,802'). Synthesized field = Permits and Fees + Direct Construction - Lot + Shared Cost Alloc.; authoritative per-lot h-cost from 2025Status.

HorzCustomer: Horizontal customer / builder code (e.g., 'BCP' for Building Construction Partners). Identifies vertical buyer identity; maps to canonical_entity via entity_crosswalk; supports lot ownership tracking.
Unnamed: 16: Unintended empty column (no header); parsing artifact from Excel export. Ignore; no data present.

Sum of Lot Count: Pivot-table summary row header or phase-level lot aggregate (string values 'Project', project names, or numeric sums). Marks reporting boundary; not a transaction-detail field—ignore for per-lot analysis.

Collateral Bucket.1: Pivot-table column header or collateral bucket distribution (string, e.g., '1 - Entitled Land' or lot count '  1,251 '). Reporting summary; not per-lot detail—use only for phase-level collateral-bucket headcount.
Unnamed: 19: Pivot-table collateral bucket column ('2 - Development WIP') or lot count (e.g., '  143 '). Reporting helper; extract numeric values for phase-level bucket distribution only.
Unnamed: 20: Pivot-table collateral bucket column ('3 - Finished Lots') or lot count. Use for phase bucket reporting only; not row-level detail.
Unnamed: 21: Pivot-table collateral bucket column ('4 - Vertical WIP') or lot count. Reporting summary; not per-transaction.
Unnamed: 22: Pivot-table collateral bucket column ('5 - Complete & Unsold') or lot count. Reporting summary; extract counts for phase bucket rollups only.
Unnamed: 23: Pivot-table collateral bucket column ('6 - Complete & Sold') or lot count. Reporting summary.
Unnamed: 24: Pivot-table collateral bucket column ('7 - Closed') or lot count. Reporting summary; extract for phase bucket analysis only.
Unnamed: 25: Pivot-table grand total row (string 'Grand Total' or numeric sums). Reporting control; validate against phase sum-of-lot-counts.
Project: Community / development name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs, Harmony). Source: Collateral Report lender package, as_of 2025-12-31. Primary key for phase-level collateral grouping.
Phase / plat identifier within the project (e.g., '123', '8', '9'). Source: Collateral Report. Whitespace-normalized via canonical phase mapping.
Product Type: Lot product category (SFR = single-family residential, or MF = multifamily). Source: Collateral Report. Drives advance-rate and valuation method selection.

Status: Development phase status (e.g., 'Vertical', 'Horizontal', 'Finished'). Source: Collateral Report. Indicates whether lots are under infrastructure development, vertical construction, or complete.
Location: Geographic location / city of the phase (e.g., Salem Utah, Eagle Mountain Utah). Source: Collateral Report. Informational only.
In Collateral Pool?: Boolean flag ('Yes' / 'No') indicating whether this phase row is pledged as collateral to the lender. Source: Collateral Report. Drives borrowing-base inclusion.
Flagship: Count of lots owned/controlled by Flagship Homes (the horizontal developer). Source: Collateral Report. Numerator for flagship-vs-external lot split.
External: Count of lots owned by external / vertical builders or partners. Source: Collateral Report. Complementary to Flagship count for collateral-pool granularity.
Total lot count (typically = Flagship + External, or standalone if no split). Source: Collateral Report. Used for per-lot cost basis and advance-rate calculations.
Check: Validation/reconciliation field (typically 0). Source: Collateral Report. Flags data anomalies in the workbook; not business data.
# of Lots: Count of lots in the phase row subject to the following collateral buckets (e.g., 76 for 'Dev not collateralized'). Source: Collateral Report. Denominator for per-lot cost rollup.

Lot Type: Classification of lot collateral status (e.g., 'Dev not collateralized', 'Finished Lots', 'Raw Land'). Source: Collateral Report. Routes lot value to appropriate advance rate and borrowing-base line.

Raw Land Allocation: Allocated raw land cost per phase (string with comma separators, e.g., ' 1,420,000 '). Source: Collateral Report cost-basis section. Component of total development cost; must be parsed to numeric.
Offsites Allocation: Allocated offsite / utility infrastructure cost per phase (string, e.g., ' 1,340,382 '). Source: Collateral Report. Shared cost routed across phase lots.

ITD Dev Costs: In-to-date (cumulative) development costs incurred through report date (string, e.g., ' 2,749,453 '). Source: Collateral Report. Actual spend; paired with remaining for total-dev-cost reconciliation.
Paper Lot Value: Base lot value assigned for appraisal / paper valuation (string, e.g., ' 35,000 '). Source: Collateral Report valuation section. Used in finished-lot value and advance-rate calcs.
Finished Lot Value: Appraised value of finished (complete) lots (string, e.g., ' 0 '). Source: Collateral Report. Zero when phase is still under development; populated for mature phases.

Value generation via dev cost: Per-lot value uplift attributed to development spend (string, e.g., ' 60,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. Markup applied to land cost to model development-stage value creation.

Total Lot Value : Calculated total lot value per phase (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$0 '). Source: Collateral Report. Sum of paper + finished + value-generation components; often zero for vertical phases.
Advance % : Lender advance rate applied to this lot type / phase bucket (string, e.g., '60.00%'). Source: Collateral Report. Drives borrowing-base limit; 0-80% range typical.
Loan $ : Calculated loan amount available against this bucket (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$0 '). Source: Collateral Report. = Total Lot Value × Advance %; often zero for non-finished lots.
Remaining Dev Costs : Remaining (forecast) development spend to completion (string, e.g., '  1,154,293.60 '). Source: Collateral Report. Budget less ITD; used for cash-flow and covenant calculations.
Total Dev Cost (Spent + Remaining) : Total expected development cost (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$0 '). Source: Collateral Report. ITD + Remaining; authority for phase total-cost reconciliation.

Remaining Dev Costs per Lot : Per-lot remaining development cost (string, often ' - ' when zero or not applicable). Source: Collateral Report. = Remaining Dev Costs / # of Lots; informational for per-lot modeling.

Total Dev Cost per Lot : Per-lot total development cost (string, often ' - ' when zero or not applicable). Source: Collateral Report. = Total Dev Cost / # of Lots; key for margin and per-lot cost basis.
All-in Cost per Lot : Per-lot all-inclusive cost (land + dev + indirect) (string, often ' - '). Source: Collateral Report. Calculated; rarely populated; used for internal cost-basis modeling.

Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot) : Average vertical construction cost per home, excluding lot cost (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$292,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. Vertical builder cost baseline; informs per-unit margin.

Total Vertical Costs for Homes : Total vertical (home construction) costs for the phase (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$22,192,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. WIP in-place; used for completion-cost and liquidity analysis.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude.xlsx_-_collateral_report. Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.field_name: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.description: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes : Sum of remaining development costs and vertical completion costs (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$22,192,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. Total cash needed to final delivery; covenant trigger.
# of Lots.1: Lot count for the next collateral bucket / status row (e.g., 25 for production WIP lots). Source: Collateral Report. Denominator for WIP-related per-lot calculations.

Production Unit WIP : Work-in-progress value of homes under vertical construction (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$7,000,855 '). Source: Collateral Report. Balance-sheet asset; portion of borrowing base for WIP collateral.

Production Unit WIP (excl. Lot) : WIP value excluding lot cost (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$4,640,005 '). Source: Collateral Report. Vertical construction cost only; used for advance-rate calculations on WIP.
As of Date: Report date / valuation date (string, e.g., '12/31/2025'). Source: Collateral Report header. Snapshot date for all collateral figures; v2.1 uses 2025-12-31.
Advance % .1: Lender advance rate for production unit WIP (string, e.g., '70.00%'). Source: Collateral Report. Higher than finished-lot rate; reflects WIP risk profile.
Loan $ .1: Loan amount available against WIP collateral (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$4,900,599 '). Source: Collateral Report. = WIP (excl. Lot) × Advance %.

Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot) .1: Average vertical cost per WIP home (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$292,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. Same as prior section; repeated for WIP bucket; no lot cost included.
Total Vertical Costs for Homes .1: Total WIP vertical costs (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$7,300,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. May differ from prior total if mix/count changes between sections.
Cost to Complete Homes : Remaining cost to finish WIP homes (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$2,659,995 '). Source: Collateral Report. Forecast; used for cash-flow and completion-timeline analysis.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude.xlsx_-_collateral_report. Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.field_name: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.description: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP : Sum of remaining lot dev costs and remaining WIP completion costs (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$24,851,995 '). Source: Collateral Report. Total cash outflow to close and complete phase.
# of Lots.2: Lot count for model homes / spec homes (string, e.g., 1). Source: Collateral Report. Typically small count; high per-unit value.
Per Model Value: Per-unit valuation for model / spec homes (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$550,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. Retail market value; used for advance-rate bucket.
Total Model Value : Total value of all model homes in phase (string with $ prefix, e.g., ' $ 550,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. = Per Model Value × # of Lots.2.
Advance % .2: Lender advance rate on model home collateral (string, e.g., '75.00%'). Source: Collateral Report. Reflects higher collateral quality of retail-ready homes.
Loan $ .2: Loan amount available against model home value (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$412,500 '). Source: Collateral Report. = Total Model Value × Advance %.
# of Lots.3: Lot count for sold homes / homes closing soon (string, e.g., 27). Source: Collateral Report. High-stage inventory; may have zero borrowing-base weight.
Per Home Value: Per-unit value for closed / sold homes (string with $ prefix, e.g., '$499,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. Market / contract price; often removed from collateral pool.
Total Home Value : Total value of closed / sold homes (string with $ prefix, e.g., ' $ 2,994,000 '). Source: Collateral Report. = Per Home Value × # of Lots.3.
Advance % .3: Lender advance rate on sold homes (string, e.g., '80.00%'). Source: Collateral Report. Often 0% or excluded; homes moving off collateral.
Loan $ .3: Loan amount against sold homes (string with $ prefix, typically '$0 '). Source: Collateral Report. Usually zero; sold homes are not collateral.
# of Lots.4: Placeholder / trailing lot count field (typically 0). Source: Collateral Report. Not used in current workbook; residual data artifact.

Per Home Value.1: Per-unit sales price or estimated value for a product type or phase within the collateral pool, formatted as a currency string from the Collateral Report lender snapshot (as_of 2025-12-31).

Total Home Value .1: Aggregate market or estimated value across all units of a product type, calculated as per-unit value × unit count; serves as the numerator for advance-rate calculations in the Collateral Report.

Advance % .4: Loan-to-value percentage (e.g., 90%) applied to the total home value to determine the lender's maximum advance for that product type or phase, per the Collateral Report borrowing-base policy.

Loan $ .4: Dollar amount of lender advance available for the product type, calculated as total home value × advance %; typically zero when collateral is not yet pledged or the pool is inactive per the Collateral Report.

# of Lots.5: Count of buildable lots or units allocated to a product type or phase within the phase-level collateral row; used as a denominator for per-lot value calculations in the Collateral Report.

Per Home Value.2: Secondary per-unit value field, likely representing an alternative product type or pricing scenario within the same phase; numeric type suggests parsing attempt but values are sparse or empty.

Total Home Value .2: Secondary total value field, typically showing zero or dash (" $ - ") when the alternative product type is not applicable or not yet marketed; supports multi-product scenarios in Collateral Report.
Advance % .5: Loan-to-value rate for the secondary product type or scenario, typically 0% when that product type carries no lender advance commitment in the Collateral Report.

Loan $ .5: Loan advance dollar amount for the secondary product type; almost always zero in samples, indicating the secondary product is either not marketed, not pledged, or a planning-only scenario per the Collateral Report.
Unnamed: 59: Unnamed column with no sample values; likely a parse artifact (empty column in source XLSX) and should be ignored or dropped during data ingestion.

Total Units : Sum of all unit counts (lots + multifamily units, if applicable) across product types within a phase or project; used to weight collateral and advance calculations in the Collateral Report.

Total Value : Aggregate collateral value across all product types and scenarios within a phase, combining primary and secondary values; numerator for the phase-level advance-rate and lender-advance calculations in the Collateral Report.

Advance % .6: Phase-level weighted loan-to-value percentage, reflecting the blend of advance rates across product types and units; used to compute total lender advance capacity per phase in the Collateral Report (as_of 2025-12-31).

Lender Advance : Phase-level dollar amount of lender advance authorized, calculated as total value × advance %; represents the borrowing-base commitment available against the pledged collateral pool per the Collateral Report lender package.
Project: Community / project name (e.g., HARMONY, PARKWAY FIELDS). Primary identifier for grouping phase-level collateral data from the prior collateral snapshot (as_of 2025-06-30).
Phase / plat label within the project (e.g., '8', '9', '10'). Combined with Project, identifies the specific development phase whose collateral position is reported.
Product Type: Product classification—primarily 'SFR' (Single-Family Residential) or 'MF' (Multifamily). Indicates lot/unit type for collateral valuation and advance-rate purposes.
Status: Current operational status of the phase (e.g., 'Vertical', 'Finished Lots', 'Finished Units'). Reflects the stage of horizontal development (land) or vertical construction.
Location: Geographic location of the project / phase (e.g., 'Eagle Mountain, Utah'). Provides spatial context for the collateral pool.
In Collateral Pool?: Boolean flag ('Yes' / 'No') indicating whether this phase is pledged as collateral to the lender. Determines if borrowing-base calculations apply.
Flagship Homes equity stake or ownership percentage in this phase. Numeric value (e.g., 48.0, 50.0). Used to determine borrowing-base allocation.
External: Third-party / external partner stake or ownership percentage in this phase. Numeric value. Complements Flagship percentage to indicate ownership split.
Total ownership percentage (sum of Flagship + External). Numeric value; should equal 100 or indicate the consolidated ownership claim for borrowing-base calculation.
Check: Validation / reconciliation column. Numeric value (typically 0.0); used to flag discrepancies between Flagship, External, and Total ownership percentages.
# of Lots: Count of finished lots or buildable parcels in this phase. Numeric value; used as denominator for per-lot cost and per-lot value calculations.
Lot Type: Classification of lot status (e.g., 'Finished Lots', 'Raw Land'). Indicates the development stage and borrowing-base treatment of the collateral.
Raw Land Allocation: Dollar amount (stored as string with thousand separators) allocated to raw / unimproved land in this phase. Component of total development cost basis.
Offsites Allocation: Dollar amount (string) allocated to off-site infrastructure (utilities, roads, easements, etc.). Portion of shared horizontal development cost.
ITD Dev Costs: Incurred-to-date development costs (string format). Cumulative horizontal / site costs spent on this phase as of the prior snapshot date (2025-06-30).
Paper Lot Value: Nominal / estimated lot value per unit (string). Used in collateral valuation models; often conservative placeholder before market pricing.
Finished Lot Value: Appraised or market value per finished lot (string). Derived from appraisals or comparable sales; zero if phase status is not 'Finished Lots'.
Value generation via dev cost: Incremental lot value attributed to development activity (string). Represents appreciation from raw to finished lot status; used in collateral pool valuation.
Total Lot Value : Aggregate value of all lots in phase (string, typically prefixed with $ symbol). Sum of per-lot values; key collateral pool metric for advance-rate calculation.
Advance % : Lender advance rate as a percentage (string, e.g., '60.00%'). Determines the maximum loan-to-value ratio for finished lots in this phase.
Loan $ : Total outstanding or available loan amount (string, $ format). Calculated as Total Lot Value × Advance %; represents borrowing-base limit for this collateral bucket.
Remaining Dev Costs : Forecasted remaining horizontal development costs to complete the phase (string, $ format). Subtracted from total to estimate finish-state value.
Total Dev Cost (Spent + Remaining) : Cumulative budgeted horizontal cost (spent + projected) for the phase (string, $ format). Total budget from raw land to finished-lot readiness.
Remaining Dev Costs per Lot : Remaining development cost divided by lot count (string). Per-lot metric; often shown as '  -   ' if lot count is zero.
Total Dev Cost per Lot : Total development budget divided by lot count (string). Per-lot cost metric for phase economics; null if lot count is zero or phase is not lot-based.
All-in Cost per Lot : All-inclusive cost per lot including land, horizontal, and indirect allocations (string). Comprehensive per-lot cost basis for financial modeling.
Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot) : Average vertical construction cost per home, excluding lot cost (string, $ format). Vertical builder cost assumption for WIP and cost-to-complete calculations.
Total Vertical Costs for Homes : Aggregate vertical construction cost for homes under construction or completed in phase (string, $ format). WIP or construction-in-progress asset.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude.xlsx_-_priorcr. Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.field_name: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.description: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes : Sum of remaining horizontal development and vertical completion costs (string, $ format). Total 'cost to completion' for lender reserve planning.
# of Lots.1: Count of homes under construction or in production (numeric). Secondary lot-count field for production-unit WIP bucket.
Production Unit WIP : Work-in-progress value for homes under vertical construction (string, $ format). Total invested cost in partially-built units.
Production Unit WIP (excl. Lot) : Production unit WIP excluding raw lot cost (string, $ format). Vertical-construction cost only; land is separately accounted.
As of Date: Snapshot date for this collateral report (string, date format e.g., '6/30/2025'). Indicates the prior-period cutoff; v2.1 uses 2025-06-30 for PriorCR.
Advance % .1: Secondary advance rate (string, % format, e.g., '70.00%'). Applies to production-unit WIP collateral bucket; typically higher than finished-lot rate.
Loan $ .1: Loan amount for production-unit WIP bucket (string, $ format). Calculated as Production Unit WIP × Advance % .1.
Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot) .1: Vertical cost per home for WIP units (string, $ format). May differ from finished-lot vertical cost if spec or lot-type mix varies.
Total Vertical Costs for Homes .1: Total vertical cost for homes in production-unit WIP bucket (string, $ format). Component of total WIP value.
Cost to Complete Homes : Remaining vertical construction cost to finish homes currently under construction (string, $ format). Reserve requirement for lender.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude.xlsx_-_priorcr. Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.field_name: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.description: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP : Sum of remaining lot development and vertical completion costs (string, $ format). Total contingency / reserve needed to completion.
# of Lots.2: Count of model homes or display units (numeric). Third collateral bucket; small count, high per-unit value.
Per Model Value: Appraised value per model home (string, $ format). Market valuation for model-home collateral pool.
Total Model Value : Aggregate value of all model homes (string, $ format). Count × Per Model Value.
Advance % .2: Advance rate for model-home collateral bucket (string, % format, e.g., '75.00%'). Typically conservative (lower) due to illiquidity and limited comparable sales.
Loan $ .2: Loan amount available against model-home collateral (string, $ format). Total Model Value × Advance % .2.
# of Lots.3: Count of completed / closed homes (numeric). Fourth collateral bucket; highest per-unit value, lowest risk.
Per Home Value: Market or contract value per closed home (string, $ format). Reflects accepted offer prices or actual sales prices.
Total Home Value : Aggregate value of closed homes (string, $ format). Count × Per Home Value; most liquid collateral pool.
Advance % .3: Advance rate for closed-home collateral bucket (string, % format, e.g., '80.00%'). Highest rate; fully completed and accepted homes represent lowest lender risk.
Loan $ .3: Loan amount available against closed-home collateral (string, $ format). Total Home Value × Advance % .3; primary borrowing-base driver.
# of Lots.4: Count of units in a fifth or residual collateral bucket (numeric). May represent pending closings, holdbacks, or specialized inventory.

Per Home Value.1: Per-unit selling price for single-family residential lots in the collateral pool snapshot as of 2025-06-30 (prior period). Formatted as currency string; used to derive total collateral value.

Total Home Value .1: Aggregate selling value across all single-family lots in the phase (per-home price × lot count). Currency-formatted string from Collateral Report prior snapshot; backing metric for lender advance calculations.

Advance % .4: Lender advance rate applied to single-family residential collateral pool, expressed as percentage. Typically 75–90%; determines loan-to-value ratio for borrowing-base computation in prior period.
Loan $ .4: Dollar amount of lender advance against single-family residential collateral (total home value × advance %). Currency string; part of the total borrowing base at prior as-of date.
# of Lots.5: Count of single-family residential buildable lots pledged in the collateral pool. Float; used to denominate per-home value and validate lot-count consistency across source snapshots.

Per Home Value.2: Per-unit value for a secondary residential product type or alternate use classification within the phase. Float; mostly empty, indicating single-product phases or deprecated data structure.
Total Home Value .2: Aggregate value for secondary residential product type. Currency string; zero-filled in prior snapshot, reflecting single-product collateral composition.
Advance % .5: Lender advance rate for secondary residential product type, if any. Percentage string; zero in prior snapshot where secondary product has no collateral pledge.
Loan $ .5: Dollar advance against secondary residential product collateral. Currency string; zero across samples, confirming single-product pledge in prior period.
Unnamed: 59: Unlabeled column; origin and business meaning unclear from samples (no values present). Likely an artifact of multi-tab merge; recommend dropping or confirming source-owner intent.

Total Units : Total count of all residential units (single-family + multifamily + alternative products) in the collateral pool phase. Float; used to compute blended advance rates and borrowing-base composition.

Total Value : Aggregate collateral value across all product types and units in the phase (sum of single-family total value + multifamily total value + other). Currency string; primary collateral-pool size metric.
Advance % .6: Blended lender advance rate applied to total phase collateral (weighted average or per-phase policy rate). Percentage string; typically 75–90%; drives total borrowing-base calculation.

Lender Advance : Total dollar amount of lender advance against the full phase collateral pool (total value × blended advance %). Currency string; the final borrowing-base commitment metric from prior period (as of 2025-06-30).

Project: Canonical community / project name (e.g. Arrowhead Springs, Harmony). Primary identifier for grouping lots and phases within a development. Source: Collateral Report lender package, as_of 2025-12-31.
Phase / plat number within the project (e.g. 8, 9, 123). Used with Project to identify the geographic development section. Source: Collateral Report.
Product Type: Housing product category (SFR = single-family residential). Indicates the unit type collateralizing this phase row. Source: Collateral Report.

Status: Collateral stage indicator (e.g. Vertical, Finished Lots, Dev not collateralized). Describes whether the phase lots are under development, complete, or excluded from the borrowing base. Source: Collateral Report.
Location: Geographic location of the phase (city, state). Provides spatial context for the community. Source: Collateral Report.
In Collateral Pool?: Yes/No flag indicating whether this phase lot pool is pledged to the lender. Determines whether the row contributes to borrowing-base calculations. Source: Collateral Report.
Flagship: Count of lots owned or controlled by Flagship Homes within this phase. Subset of total lot count; used to calculate ownership-weighted borrowing base. Source: Collateral Report.
External: Count of lots owned or controlled by external / third-party builders within this phase. Complements Flagship count to reach total lot pool. Source: Collateral Report.
Total lot count (Flagship + External) for this phase. Denominator for per-lot cost and collateral calculations. Source: Collateral Report.
Check: Reconciliation field; should equal Total - Flagship - External (validation check for data integrity). Source: Collateral Report.
# of Lots: Number of lots in this phase collateral pool row. May differ from Total if the row represents a sub-segment (e.g. Dev not collateralized). Source: Collateral Report.
Lot Type: Collateral classification of the lot pool (e.g. Dev not collateralized, Finished Lots, Raw Land). Drives advance rate and borrowing-base treatment. Source: Collateral Report.
Raw Land Allocation: Dollar amount of raw land acquisition cost allocated to this phase pool. Component of total development cost basis. Source: Collateral Report.
Offsites Allocation: Dollar amount of infrastructure / offsite development cost (roads, utilities, engineering) allocated to this phase. Source: Collateral Report.
ITD Dev Costs: In-to-date (accumulated through as_of_date) development costs spent on this phase. Actual spend against budget. Source: Collateral Report.
Paper Lot Value: Baseline per-lot land value before development cost is factored in. Used in collateral valuation formulas. Source: Collateral Report.
Finished Lot Value: Per-lot value after all development / horizontal work is complete. Higher than Paper Lot Value; used for finished-lot collateral classification. Source: Collateral Report.
Value generation via dev cost: Dollar amount or multiplier representing value added per unit of development cost spent. Reflects development-cost-to-value conversion. Source: Collateral Report.
Total Lot Value: Total collateral value for all lots in this phase pool (sum of per-lot value × lot count). Primary driver of borrowing base. Source: Collateral Report.
Advance %: Lender advance rate against this phase pool's total lot value (e.g. 0.6 = 60% LTV). Determines maximum loan $ available. Source: Collateral Report.
Loan $: Current outstanding loan amount against this phase pool at the specified advance %. May be zero if not yet drawn. Source: Collateral Report.
Remaining Dev Costs: Forecasted development cost still to be spent on this phase to reach completion. Budget minus ITD Dev Costs. Source: Collateral Report.
Total Dev Cost (Spent + Remaining): Complete budgeted development cost for this phase (ITD Dev Costs + Remaining Dev Costs). Total horizontal / development scope. Source: Collateral Report.
Remaining Dev Costs per Lot: Per-lot remaining development cost (Remaining Dev Costs ÷ # of Lots). Used to project cost to completion on a per-lot basis. Source: Collateral Report.
Total Dev Cost per Lot: Per-lot total development cost (Total Dev Cost ÷ # of Lots). Benchmark cost basis per residential lot. Source: Collateral Report.
All-in Cost per Lot: Total cost per lot including land + development + vertical (home construction). Comprehensive cost basis for margin analysis. Source: Collateral Report.
Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot): Average vertical / construction cost per home excluding lot and horizontal work. Building cost only. Source: Collateral Report.
Total Vertical Costs for Homes: Total value of vertical (home construction) work-in-progress on homes in this phase pool. Asset backing for vertical WIP collateral. Source: Collateral Report.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude_(1).collateral_report.Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.field_name: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.description: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes: Sum of remaining development cost + vertical cost to finish homes. Remaining spend to complete all units. Source: Collateral Report.
# of Lots.1: Recount of lots in a secondary collateral pool segment or production unit bucket. Distinguishes from primary lot count. Source: Collateral Report.
Production Unit WIP: Work-in-progress value of homes under construction (vertical WIP) in this phase. Total value of all homes in production. Source: Collateral Report.
Production Unit WIP (excl. Lot): Vertical WIP value excluding the land / lot cost component. Pure construction cost in progress. Source: Collateral Report.
As of Date: Snapshot date for this collateral report row (2025-12-31). All figures are as of this date; enables historical comparison. Source: Collateral Report.
Advance %.1: Secondary advance rate (often higher, e.g. 0.7 = 70%) applied to a different collateral bucket or WIP pool. Represents lender appetite for this asset class. Source: Collateral Report.
Loan $.1: Loan amount advanced at the secondary advance rate. May apply to WIP or finished-lot pools with different LTV. Source: Collateral Report.
Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot).1: Restatement of average vertical cost per home for a secondary pool or segment. Confirms cost consistency across buckets. Source: Collateral Report.
Total Vertical Costs for Homes.1: Total vertical WIP value for a secondary production unit or collateral segment. Source: Collateral Report.
Cost to Complete Homes: Remaining construction cost to finish all homes currently in WIP. Budget for vertical completion. Source: Collateral Report.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude_(1).collateral_report.Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.field_name: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.description: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP: Sum of remaining lot development cost + cost to complete homes. Total remaining spend for both horizontal and vertical work. Source: Collateral Report.
# of Lots.2: Lot count for a tertiary collateral pool segment or model type bucket. Further subdivides the phase pool. Source: Collateral Report.
Per Model Value: Per-unit value assigned to a specific home model (e.g. $550,000 per model home). Used in finished-model-home collateral sections. Source: Collateral Report.
Total Model Value: Total collateral value for all model homes of this type (Per Model Value × # of model homes). Source: Collateral Report.
Advance %.2: Tertiary advance rate applied to model homes (e.g. 0.75 = 75%). Often higher than lot advance due to tangible collateral. Source: Collateral Report.
Loan $.2: Loan amount advanced against model-home collateral at the tertiary advance rate. Source: Collateral Report.
# of Lots.3: Lot count for a quaternary collateral pool segment (e.g. finished homes for sale). Source: Collateral Report.
Per Home Value: Per-unit value for completed, finished homes (e.g. $499,000 per home). Expected sales value or appraised value. Source: Collateral Report.
Total Home Value: Total collateral value for finished homes (Per Home Value × # of finished homes). Source: Collateral Report.
Advance %.3: Quaternary advance rate applied to finished homes (e.g. 0.8 = 80%). Typically highest LTV due to completed, marketable collateral. Source: Collateral Report.
Loan $.3: Loan amount advanced against finished-home collateral at the quaternary advance rate. Source: Collateral Report.
# of Lots.4: Lot count for a fifth collateral pool segment (may be zero if unused bucket). Final pool tier. Source: Collateral Report.
Per Home Value.1: Per-unit sales price for the first product type or phase segment within a collateral phase row. Typically $499K–$850K; feeds into total home value calculation.
Total Home Value.1: Aggregate sales value across all units in the first product-type segment (per-unit price × unit count). Ranges from $850K to $3.8M; used to compute lender advance eligibility.
Advance %.4: Lender advance rate (loan-to-value ratio) applied to the first product-type segment, expressed as a decimal (0.9 = 90%). Determines maximum borrowing base on collateral value.

Loan $.4: Dollar amount of lender advance available against the first product-type segment at the stated advance rate. All samples show $0, indicating no active borrowing or zero available capacity on this segment.
# of Lots.5: Lot count for a phase or product-type segment; ranges from 0 to 50 lots. Used as the denominator for per-lot cost and pricing calculations within the collateral snapshot.
Per Home Value.2: Per-unit sales price for a second product type or phase variant. Samples are empty; indicates this column is either unused or applies to a minority of collateral rows.
Total Home Value.2: Aggregate sales value for the second product-type segment. Single sample is $0; suggests this segment is inactive or reserved for future phase mix.
Advance %.5: Lender advance rate for the second product-type segment. All samples are 0.0, consistent with zero or inactive borrowing on secondary product types.
Loan $.5: Dollar loan amount on the second product-type segment. All samples are $0, indicating no active advance or zero available capacity on this secondary segment.
Unnamed: 59: Unidentified column (parsing artifact); no meaningful data or description available. Likely a stray merged-cell boundary or formatting artifact in the source XLSX.
Total Units: Total buildable unit count across all product types and phases within the collateral row. Ranges from 0 to 129 units; aggregates single-family and multifamily inventory.

Total Value: Aggregate collateral value across all units and product types. Samples show mixed numeric/text storage (e.g., 7.55M, 499K, 0); indicates a column intended as currency but parsed as string due to formatting inconsistency.
Advance %.6: Blended or overall lender advance rate across all product types and units in the phase. Ranges from 0 to ~70%, reflecting weighted or composite loan-to-value eligibility.

Lender Advance: Total dollar amount the lender will advance against the entire phase collateral package at the blended advance rate. Ranges from $0 to $5.3M; represents actual or available borrowing capacity.
Unnamed: 0: Row index or sequence number; appears empty across sampled rows.
Unnamed: 1: Collateral pool category label (e.g., 'Land + WIP + Homes Collateral'). Identifies the asset class or collateral grouping being valued for lender borrowing-base calculation.
Unnamed: 2: Collateral-bucket subcategory or lot lifecycle stage within the pool (e.g., 'Sold, But Not Closed', 'Finished Homes', 'Model Homes'). Drives advance-rate weighting in RBA calculations.
Unnamed: 3: Numeric field; appears empty across sampled rows. Likely a placeholder or intermediate calculation column.
Unnamed: 4: Numeric field; appears empty across sampled rows. Likely a placeholder or intermediate calculation column.

Unnamed: 6: Asset valuation method label ('At Value') or corresponding dollar amount per collateral bucket. Records the lender-appraised or market value of collateral pool used in RBA advance-rate application.

Unnamed: 7: Assumed advance rate (e.g., 0.9, 0.8) or advance-rate label ('Assumed Adv Rate'). Percentage of appraised collateral value available for borrowing against this pool in the Collateral Report RBA-TNW tab.

Unnamed: 8: Potential borrowing base dollar amount or label ('Potential Borrowing Base'). Calculated as At Value × Assumed Adv Rate per collateral bucket; feeds lender loan-sizing and financial covenant analysis.

Project: Community / project name from the 2025 operational status workbook. Values like 'Arrowhead Springs' identify which residential development this lot belongs to. Source: internal 2025Status workbook.
Phase / plat number within the project, stored as numeric identifier (e.g., 123). Combined with Project and Lot, forms the unique lot location within a development. Source: 2025Status workbook.
Lot number within the phase, numeric (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Together with Project and Phase, uniquely identifies a buildable parcel. Source: 2025Status workbook.
Product Type: Housing type classification, typically 'SFR' (single-family residential). Indicates the intended use category for this lot. Source: 2025Status workbook.
Lot Count: Count of lots represented by this row, typically 1 for individual lot entries. Used in lot-level summaries and phase-level aggregations. Source: 2025Status workbook.

Status: Lifecycle stage code and label (e.g., '13 - Vert Purchase', '15 - Vert CO'). Encodes the lot's progress through the development waterfall from land purchase through construction completion and closing. Source: 2025Status workbook; derived from lifecycle date waterfall.

Status Date: Date associated with the current status code; represents when the lot reached this stage (e.g., vertical purchase or certificate of occupancy date). Timestamp in ISO 8601 format. Source: 2025Status workbook.

Vert Sold: Boolean indicator ('Yes'/'No') whether the vertical unit (completed house) has been sold to an end buyer. Distinguishes sold-but-not-closed from unsold finished inventory. Source: 2025Status workbook.

Collateral Bucket: Lender collateral classification (e.g., '3 - Finished Lots', '5 - Complete & Unsold') that groups lots by development stage for loan advance-rate calculations. Drives borrowing-base valuation. Source: 2025Status workbook; derived from lot lifecycle stage.

Permits and Fees: Horizontal development cost allocation for permits and entitlement fees, in USD. This per-lot component of total horizontal cost accumulates permitting, engineering, and similar enabling costs. Source: 2025Status workbook.

Direct Construction - Lot: Lot-specific direct construction cost (e.g., site preparation, grading), in USD. Allocated directly to this lot; zero for lots not yet in vertical construction. Source: 2025Status workbook.
Direct Construction: Total direct construction cost (horizontal + vertical), in USD. May be larger than 'Direct Construction - Lot' if shared infrastructure is allocated. Source: 2025Status workbook.

Vertical Costs: Vertical construction costs specific to the house structure (framing, plumbing, electrical, finishes), in USD. Incurred by the vertical builder or subcontractor. Source: 2025Status workbook.

Shared Cost Alloc.: Allocation of shared phase or community-level indirect costs (amenities, roadway common areas, utilities) to this specific lot, in USD. Typically a small per-lot share of total. Source: 2025Status workbook.
Lot Cost: Net per-lot development cost basis; typically mirrors 'Direct Construction - Lot' for cost allocation purposes. Zero when lot is not yet in vertical stage. Source: 2025Status workbook.

HorzCustomer: Horizontal (land development) customer / builder code (e.g., 'BCP' = Building Construction Partners). Identifies the entity responsible for horizontal infrastructure and lot development. Source: 2025Status workbook.
Unnamed: 16: Empty or placeholder column with no header in the source workbook. Typically a data-entry error or formatting artifact. Source: 2025Status workbook structure.

Sum of Lot Count: Pivot-table row label ('Row Labels') or project name ('Arrowhead Springs', 'Harmony') indicating the grouping level in a cross-tabulated summary. Not a computed field; artifact of pivot layout. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.

Column Labels: Pivot-table column header or collateral bucket label (e.g., '1 - Entitled Land') with count values (e.g., 1251, 137). Part of a lot-count matrix summarizing distribution across lifecycle stages. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.

Unnamed: 19: Pivot column for collateral bucket '2 - Development WIP' with lot counts per project. Represents lots in active horizontal or vertical development. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.
Unnamed: 20: Pivot column for collateral bucket '3 - Finished Lots' with lot counts per project. Represents completed, unsold, spec-inventory lots. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.
Unnamed: 21: Pivot column for collateral bucket '4 - Vertical WIP' with lot counts per project. Represents lots under vertical construction. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.
Unnamed: 22: Pivot column for collateral bucket '5 - Complete & Unsold' with lot counts per project. Represents sold-to-lender-but-not-closed lots. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.
Unnamed: 23: Pivot column for collateral bucket '6 - Complete & Sold' with lot counts per project. Represents units sold to end buyers but not yet closed. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.

Unnamed: 24: Pivot column for collateral bucket '7 - Closed' with lot counts per project. Represents closed transactions; lot ownership transferred, development cycle complete. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.

Unnamed: 25: Pivot grand-total column summing all lot counts across all collateral buckets, per project or overall. Reconciles against project lot inventory. Source: 2025Status workbook pivot structure.

Project: Community identifier from the prior-period collateral snapshot (as_of 2025-06-30). Matches canonical_project values (e.g., HARMONY, Parkway Fields). Used to join against current-period collateral and lot/phase inventory.

Phase or plat label within the project, normalized whitespace per v1 discipline. Numeric and alphanumeric phases (e.g., 8, 9, 10, A10) identify subdivisions within a community for cost and collateral tracking.
Product Type: Product category: SFR (single-family residential), MF (multifamily), or other development type. Drives advance-rate and valuation-method selection in collateral models.
Status: Lifecycle status (Vertical, Horizontal In-Progress, Finished Lots, etc.). Signals development stage and collateral bucket for advance-rate calculation per v1 LOT_STATE_TO_COLLATERAL_BUCKET.
Location: Geographic jurisdiction or site address (e.g., Eagle Mountain, Utah). Operational reference; not used in canonical cost rollups.
In Collateral Pool?: Yes/No flag indicating whether this phase is pledged to the lender. Determines if phase appears in borrowing-base calculations; all rows in this export carry Yes.
Flagship: Percentage ownership or cost share attributable to Flagship Homes entity (0–100 scale, e.g., 48.0 = 48%). Used to allocate shared costs and collateral values across multiple developers.
External: Percentage ownership or cost share attributable to external / partner entity. Complements Flagship %; together they allocate 100% of phase economics.
Total ownership / cost-share percentage for this row (often equals Flagship if External is zero or null). Reconciliation field to validate ownership split sums to 100%.
Check: Validation or reconciliation field (typically 0.0, indicating no discrepancy). May flag data-quality issues if non-zero; carry-through from collateral template.
# of Lots: Count of finished / developable lots in this phase+product-type row. Denominator for per-lot cost calculations; zero indicates phase not yet inventory-confirmed or lot count incomplete.
Lot Type: Classification of lots (Finished Lots, Paper Lots, Raw Land, etc.). Determines valuation method and collateral inclusion; finished lots carry higher borrowing-base weight.
Raw Land Allocation: Allocated share of raw land acquisition cost (in USD) for this phase, computed as total raw land cost ÷ lot count or other allocation rule. Feeds into per-lot cost basis.
Offsites Allocation: Allocated share of offsite infrastructure cost (roads, utilities, common areas outside lot boundaries) in USD. Phase-level cost spread across lot denominator.
ITD Dev Costs: In-to-date (cumulative) development costs already spent, in USD. Actual cost from GL / VF as of collateral snapshot date (2025-06-30).
Paper Lot Value: Estimated value per paper lot (unimproved, pre-infrastructure) in USD. Template assumption used in absence of actual sales comps; typically $35K–$200K range.

Finished Lot Value: Estimated value per finished lot (infrastructure complete, ready for vertical) in USD. Often zero in collateral template if not yet applicable; overridden by actual sales data when available.

Value generation via dev cost: Estimated value created by development spending (uplift from raw land to finished lot), expressed in USD or as a formula result. Bridges raw-land cost to finished-lot market value.

Total Lot Value: Total estimated collateral value of all lots in this phase+product-type (= finished lot value × lot count or sum of individual lot values). Often zero if finished-lot value is not yet populated.
Advance %: Lender advance rate as a decimal (e.g., 0.6 = 60%). Percentage of total lot value the lender will advance as a loan; varies by status / lot type per credit facility.
Loan $: Loan amount available against lot collateral = total lot value × advance %, in USD. Zero when lot count or total lot value is zero or not yet financed.
Remaining Dev Costs: Forecasted cost to complete development (finish infrastructure, permit, record lots), in USD. Forward-looking; based on allocation workbook or GL trend.

Total Dev Cost (Spent + Remaining): Total budgeted development cost (ITD + Remaining), in USD. Often zero in this collateral snapshot if only ITD or remaining is populated separately; reconciliation against allocation workbook.
Remaining Dev Costs per Lot: Remaining cost per lot (= remaining dev costs ÷ lot count), in USD. Zero when lot count is zero or remaining costs undefined.
Total Dev Cost per Lot: Total development cost per lot (= total dev cost ÷ lot count), in USD. Key metric for per-lot cost basis; zero when denominator or total is unpopulated.
All-in Cost per Lot: Sum of land cost + dev cost per lot, in USD. Represents full horizontal cost per lot before vertical construction; zero when components are zero.

Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot): Average vertical construction cost per home excluding land/lot cost, in USD (e.g., 268K). Template assumption; drives total-home-value calculations when multiplied by unit count.
Total Vertical Costs for Homes: Total vertical construction cost for all homes in this phase (= per-home cost × home count), in USD. Often zero if no homes-in-progress yet.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude_(1).priorcr.Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.field_name: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes
datasources.description: Total Dev Costs + Vertical Costs to Complete Homes: Sum of remaining development costs + vertical costs to complete all units, in USD. Measures full funding requirement for phase completion.

# of Lots.1: Secondary lot count (may represent finished lots or homes-in-progress subset). Often zero; deduplication note: multiple LotsX fields exist due to collateral template multi-section layout.
Production Unit WIP: Work-in-progress value of homes under vertical construction, in USD. Includes land + dev costs + partial vertical cost of units not yet complete.
Production Unit WIP (excl. Lot): WIP value of homes excluding lot cost, in USD. Pure vertical-construction-stage value; used in collateral calculations separate from finished-lot collateral.
As of Date: Snapshot timestamp (2025-06-30 for prior-period collateral). Distinguishes prior-period data from current-period; enables delta computation against current Collateral Report.
Advance %.1: Secondary advance rate (WIP section), as decimal. May differ from Advance % if WIP carries a different lender rate than finished lots.
Loan $.1: Loan amount available on WIP collateral (= WIP value × Advance %.1), in USD. Complements finished-lot loan amount.
Avg Cost per Home (excl. Lot).1: Vertical cost assumption for WIP homes, in USD. May differ from Avg Cost per Home if WIP represents a different product mix.
Total Vertical Costs for Homes.1: Total vertical cost for WIP homes, in USD. Component of WIP value calculation.
Cost to Complete Homes: Remaining vertical cost to complete homes currently in WIP, in USD. Forecast; used to project total funding requirement.

datasources.id: collateral_dec2025_01_claude_(1).priorcr.Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.field_name: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP
datasources.description: Total cost to complete for Lots and Vertical WIP: Sum of remaining lot dev costs + remaining vertical costs, in USD. Full forward-funding need for the phase.
# of Lots.2: Home count for model home / spec inventory section (separate collateral calculation track), in units. Often 1–7 range for model inventory.
Per Model Value: Estimated market value per model/spec home, in USD (e.g., 425K–500K). Used to collateralize completed-inventory homes.
Total Model Value: Total market value of all model/spec homes (= per-model value × model count), in USD. Collateral pool for completed inventory.
Advance %.2: Advance rate on model/spec home collateral, as decimal (typically 0.75 = 75%). Higher than raw-lot rate due to completed-home status.
Loan $.2: Loan amount available on model/spec homes (= total model value × advance %.2), in USD. Component of total borrowing base.
# of Lots.3: Home count for actively-selling spec homes (homes under contract or completed but not yet closed), in units.
Per Home Value: Market value per sold/under-contract home, in USD (e.g., 499K–533K). Reflects actual or near-term sale pricing.
Total Home Value: Total collateral value of sold/under-contract homes, in USD. Highest-priority collateral pool within borrowing-base calculation.
Advance %.3: Advance rate on sold/under-contract homes, as decimal (typically 0.8 = 80%). Highest rate due to near-certain cash flow.
Loan $.3: Loan amount available on sold/under-contract homes, in USD. Primary component of working-capital borrowing base.
# of Lots.4: Final lot-count or home-count field (collateral template may have additional detail rows). Often low count; use alongside other Lots fields to complete phase universe.
Per Home Value.1: Prior-period (as_of 2025-06-30) per-unit home value for the first collateral bucket or product type, in USD. Used to compute total collateral pool value.
Total Home Value.1: Prior-period aggregate home value for the first collateral bucket or product type (per-unit value × lot count), in USD. Component of phase-level borrowing base calculation.

Advance %.4: Prior-period lender advance rate (as decimal 0–1) applied to the first collateral bucket. Determines loan amount as a percentage of collateral value; typically 0.75–0.90 for BCPD pledged phases.
Loan $.4: Prior-period loan amount (USD) funded against the first collateral bucket, computed as total home value × advance %. Reflects lender's actual advance position as of 2025-06-30.
# of Lots.5: Prior-period lot count for the first collateral bucket (numeric denominator for per-unit calculations). Must match lot inventory to validate collateral spreadsheet integrity.
Per Home Value.2: Prior-period per-unit home value for a second collateral bucket or product type (e.g., alternative lot mix or phase subset), in USD. Typically empty for single-bucket phases.
Total Home Value.2: Prior-period aggregate home value for the second collateral bucket, in USD. Zero or null for phases with single product type; present only if multi-bucket phase layout.
Advance %.5: Prior-period lender advance rate for the second collateral bucket (as decimal). Zero or null if bucket is not present; otherwise used to compute second bucket's loan amount.
Loan $.5: Prior-period loan amount (USD) against the second collateral bucket. Zero or null if bucket is inactive; summed with first bucket loan to derive total phase-level advance.
Unnamed: 59: Unmapped column (likely a blank or separator column in the source XLSX). Data type and semantic meaning are unclear from available metadata; appears to carry no substantive data.
Total Units: Prior-period total lot count across all buckets for the phase (sum of bucket-level lot counts). Primary denominator for per-lot cost basis and collateral pool rollup.

Total Value: Prior-period aggregate collateral value (sum of all bucket home values), in USD. Stored as string in source but semantically numeric; used as lender's total pool valuation as of 2025-06-30.
Advance %.6: Prior-period blended lender advance rate across all buckets (weighted or composite), as decimal. Reflects overall loan-to-value position; used to compute total lender advance below.

Lender Advance: Prior-period total loan amount (USD) funded to the phase across all buckets, computed as total value × blended advance %. Direct measure of lender's cash position as of 2025-06-30; paired with current-period snapshot for delta analysis.

Unnamed: 0: Row index or sequence number; appears empty across sample. Used internally for row ordering in the balance-sheet export.
Unnamed: 1: Balance-sheet line-item label (e.g. 'ASSETS', 'Cash', 'Escrow/Reimbursables'). Hierarchical account or section header from the combined balance-sheet workbook.
Unnamed: 2: Sub-account or allocation detail (e.g. 'o/w Finished Lots', 'o/w Lots Under Development', 'o/w Paper Lots'). Describes lot-development stage breakdown within a parent balance-sheet line.
Unnamed: 3: Numeric balance-sheet value; typically a dollar amount corresponding to the line item. Appears empty in samples; likely carries consolidated or entity-specific account balances.
Unnamed: 4: Numeric balance-sheet value; typically a dollar amount for a second reporting entity or adjustment column. Appears empty in samples.
Unnamed: 5: Numeric balance-sheet value; typically a dollar amount for a third reporting entity or tier. Appears empty in samples.
Unnamed: 6: Numeric balance-sheet value; typically a dollar amount for a fourth reporting entity or tier. Appears empty in samples.
