Unnamed: 8: Entity name or balance value (e.g. 'Adjustments', amounts like -21884018.49, -16177.52). Column mixes labels and adjustments; represents a second-entity or adjustment-type balance-sheet column.

Unnamed: 9: Entity name or balance value (e.g. 'BCP (Flagship) Adj.', amounts like 622984.84, 0). Column carries adjusted balances or a third-entity balance-sheet slice (likely adjusted Flagship or BCPD).
Unnamed: 10: Numeric balance-sheet value; appears empty across samples. Likely a placeholder or blank tier in the multi-entity column structure.

Unnamed: 11: Entity name or balance value (e.g. 'BCPDev', amounts like 9864101.22, 100000). Column carries balances for a separate development entity (BCPDev, a Flagship affiliate per source context).
Unnamed: 12: Numeric balance-sheet value; appears empty across samples. Likely a blank tier or placeholder column in the multi-entity layout.
Unnamed: 13: Entity name or balance value (e.g. 'Consolidated', amounts like 10487086.06, 100000). Column carries consolidated (summed or combined) balance-sheet totals across all entities.

Unnamed: 0: Balance-sheet line-item label (asset/liability category or GL account name). Values include ASSETS, Current Assets, Checking/Savings, indicating chart-of-accounts structure from BCPD QB register.

2025-12-31 00:00:00: QB trial-balance amount as of 2025-12-31 (BCPD quarter-end snapshot). Signed numeric; positive values are assets/debits, negative values are liabilities/credits. Source: QuickBooks BCPD entity export.

Adjustments: Manual or system adjustment applied to the 2025-12-31 QB balance to reach collateral-reporting basis. Negative values indicate reductions (e.g., reclassifications, contra-entries). Supports lender reconciliation of GAAP QB vs. collateral pool accounting.

Inventory Asset Adj: Inventory-specific balance-sheet adjustment (typically $0.0 across rows shown, indicating no periodic inventory revaluation in this period). Distinct from the broader Adjustments column; isolated for audit trail.

Lennar/Finished Lot Adj: Adjustment for Lennar-vertical and finished-lot inventory classification. Positive values increase asset basis (e.g., reclassifying sold/finished lots into borrowing base), negative values reduce. Bridges operational lot lifecycle (Lennar close, finished-lot status) into balance-sheet collateral treatment.

Unnamed: 5: Derived or intermediate calculation (column label missing from source; semantics inferred from context). Appears to carry adjusted asset values; mix of $0.0 and positive balances suggests cumulative or subtotal role in the collateral reconciliation.

Unnamed: 6: Mapping directive or lookup table key ('Mapping to Collateral Report', 'Lot Type', 'Lots Under Development'). Labels the right-side cross-reference table structure; indicates which balance-sheet line maps to collateral-report categories.

Unnamed: 7: Cross-reference project name (right-side mapping table). Values: 'Project', 'Kirkham', canonical project identifiers from Lot Data. Links balance-sheet inventory assets to specific BCPD projects for collateral-pool allocation.

Unnamed: 8: Cross-reference phase identifier (right-side mapping table). Values: 'Phase', 'ADB13', 'ADB14' (VF project-code phase notation). Completes the project-phase join key for mapping balance-sheet line items to specific development phases.
Unnamed: 9: Sparse or empty numeric field (no sample values provided; appears unused in current snapshot). Placeholder column in the balance-sheet extract; retained for schema completeness.

Unnamed: 10: Unentitled-land project identifier (second mapping table, left section). Values: 'Project', 'AMMON', 'BRIDLE PATH' (canonical project names from Lot Data). Segregates raw-land / unentitled inventory in balance-sheet collateral basis.

Unnamed: 11: Lot-type classification (second mapping table). Values: 'Lot Type', 'Unentitled land' (raw land status per Lot Data or 2025Status collateral bucket). Used to route balance-sheet asset lines to the correct collateral pool (entitled vs. raw).

Cost of Land/: Cost basis for unentitled land / raw improvements (second mapping table, amount column). Signed numeric or text label. Carries land acquisition + pre-entitlement infrastructure; bridges balance-sheet 'Land' or 'Land Under Development' to collateral-report 'Cost of Land/Improvements' line.

Unnamed: 13: Adjustment amount or contra-account for unentitled-land basis (second mapping table). Values: text label 'Adjustments' or numeric ($0, negative adjustments). Mirrors the left-side Adjustments logic; allows independent reclassification of raw-land collateral.

Deposits: Customer deposits received / deferred revenue (second mapping table). Negative values reflect deposits held as liabilities (per GAAP liability classification). Reduces net collateral value per lender policy (deposits are borrower proceeds, not BCPD equity).

Unnamed: 15: Notes payable or debt associated with unentitled-land collateral pool (second mapping table). Negative values (liabilities). Used to compute net borrowing-base equity (cost − deposits − notes = net collateral value).

Unnamed: 16: Net collateral value per unentitled-land line item (second mapping table, rightmost column). Arithmetic result: cost − adjustments − deposits − notes payable. Feeds lender's advance-rate calculation and borrowing-base position statement.

Unnamed: 0: Account hierarchy label combining account code, fund/project name, and fund type (e.g., '111-120 · Salem Fields PID 2024A-2 Fund'). Serves as the primary transaction grouping key in this QuickBooks register extract.
Type: Transaction type classification from QuickBooks (e.g., 'Deposit', 'Check', 'Journal Entry'). Indicates the form of the cash or accounting movement within the BCPD GL register.
Date: Transaction posting date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Specifies when the movement occurred in the BCPD QuickBooks account.

Num: QuickBooks transaction reference number or identifier. May contain alphanumeric codes (e.g., 'wire') for wire transfers or numeric check numbers; used for transaction traceability and reconciliation.
Name: Counterparty or payee abbreviation (e.g., 'Salem' for Salem Fields project). Provides a shortened human-readable label for the transaction originator or destination.
Memo: Narrative description or purpose of the transaction (e.g., 'Draw', 'Salem Fields PID draw'). Contains business context explaining the nature or authorization of the movement.
Class: QuickBooks class or cost-center allocation code. Field is sparse (no sample values populated) and represents optional secondary transaction categorization or profit-center assignment.
Clr: Reconciliation status indicator; 'X' marks cleared transactions. Used in bank-account reconciliation workflows to distinguish posted vs. pending items.
Split: Receiving or offsetting account code and name (e.g., '110-104 · Checking'). Identifies the GL account on the opposite side of the double-entry transaction pair.
Debit: Amount debited to the account in USD. Positive numeric values represent increases to asset or expense accounts; zero when the transaction is credit-only or a subtotal row.
Credit: Amount credited to the account in USD. Positive numeric values represent decreases to asset accounts or increases to liability/equity accounts; typically balances against debit amounts.

Balance: Running cumulative balance after each transaction in USD. Negative values indicate net credit position (typical for liability or AP-style accounts); computed as prior balance + debit − credit.

Customer/Job/Phase: Community, project, or phase identifier from the collateral package (e.g., 'Ammon Idaho', 'Parkway Fields E-1'). Links the inventory asset and GL allocation rows to operational units for lender reporting.

132-187 · Inventory Asset: Lot-level or phase-level inventory asset value (in USD) from QB GL account 132-187, representing the balance-sheet carrying value of developed land and improvements pledged as collateral as of the reporting date.
TOTAL: Sum or rollup of inventory asset value for the row's Customer/Job/Phase; matches the 132-187 account balance when the breakdown is exhaustive (single-column total for lender tieout).

Alloc GL Account: Destination or source QB GL account code (e.g., '132-127 · Parkway E1') that receives or holds the allocation of inventory assets; used by lender to map asset value from the general ledger sub-account structure back to the collateral pool.

Building Construction Partners, LLC: Entity header and document metadata row. Contains report title ('Balance Sheet'), as-of date ('As at December 31,2025'), and division filter ('Divisions: Corporate'). Source-native from BCPD balance-sheet export.

1000: Current-month period label and numeric column. Header indicates 'Current Month' / 'DEC 2025'; data rows carry December 2025 balance-sheet amounts. Mixes header text with financial figures (e.g., 622,984.84).

Unnamed: 2: Prior-month period column. Header '     Prior Month'; carries prior-period balance-sheet balances for month-over-month comparison. Includes ISO timestamp ('2026-02-18T00:00:00') in header region; data cells hold numeric amounts.
Unnamed: 3: Two-months-prior period column. Header '     2 mo. Prior' with page indicator 'Page:    1'. Contains balance-sheet figures from two months before current period for trend analysis.

Unnamed: 4: Three-months-prior period column. Header '     3 mo. Prior'. Holds historical balance-sheet balances (e.g., 15,732,773.51) for quarterly trend visibility; includes zero entries for accounts without activity.
Unnamed: 5: Prior-year comparison column. Header '      Prior Year'. Carries December 2024 balance-sheet figures (e.g., 672,875.76) to enable year-over-year variance analysis.

Unnamed: 6: Large numeric column (float type). Sample value 137,209,664.82 suggests a major balance-sheet aggregate or consolidated total. Sparse population (one sample); semantics unclear from header labeling in source export.

Unnamed: 7: Cost-category or account-description column. Values: 'Permits and Fees', 'Direct Construction', 'Direct Construction - Lot'. Classifies horizontal development cost buckets as they appear on the balance sheet or in collateral schedules.

Unnamed: 8: Lot-state bucket label and numeric column. Header 'Lots'; values include 'Finished lots' and numeric cost (6,079,840.87). Likely summarizes cost or count for the finished-lot inventory state.

Unnamed: 9: Development-stage indicator and mixed-content column. Values: 'Dev', 'Financial data through 3/31/2025', 'Finished lots'. Appears to be a narrative/label column indicating cost category or reporting-period qualifier.

Unnamed: 10: Work-in-progress (WIP) and vertical-started cost column. Header 'WIP'; values: 'Vertical started', 8,207,020.85. Aggregates cost for homes in vertical construction (foundation through framing).

Unnamed: 11: Vertical-complete cost column. Header 'Complete'; values: 'Vertical complete', 5,976,054.25. Holds accumulated cost for homes where vertical construction is finished (ready for sale/closing).
Unnamed: 12: Sold-not-closed cost column. Header 'Sold'; values: 'Home sold', 202,360.21. Tracks cost basis or carrying value for homes sold but not yet closed (in escrow/settlement phase).

Unnamed: 13: Sale-closed or realized cost column. Header 'Closed'; values: 'Sale closed', near-zero floating-point (−5.46e-11). May represent residual cost or gain/loss realized at closing; near-zero value suggests reconciliation target.

Unnamed: 14: Total cost-by-stage column. Header 'Total'; values: 14,395,778.59, 61,738,096.06. Sums the lot-state buckets (finished, WIP, vertical-complete, sold, closed) to produce per-category or per-project total cost.

Unnamed: 15: Month-over-month or period variance column. Header 'Difference'; values: −558,936.10, −17,921,690.51. Carries the delta between current period and prior period (or budget variance); negative values indicate cost reduction or favorable variance.

Project: Community / development name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs, Harmony). Source of truth for project identity across Lot Data; pairs with Phase and LotNo. to form the lot 3-tuple key used in cost allocation and lifecycle state derivation.

Phase: Numeric phase identifier within the project (e.g., 123 for Arrowhead Springs Phase 123). Combined with Project and LotNo. to uniquely identify a lot; authoritative for v1 lot-state waterfall and phase-level cost rollups.

ProdType: Product type classification (e.g., SFR = single-family residential). Drives lot-mix variance analysis in allocation workbooks and collateral advance-rate weighting; sourced from internal lot-type metadata.

HorzSeller: Horizontal developer entity code (e.g., ASD, BCPD, BCPBL, BCPI). Authoritative source for canonical_entity; identifies which partner entity executed land development. Maps to canonical_entity via entity crosswalk.

HorzCustomer: Vertical builder / purchaser entity code (e.g., BCP, Lennar, EXT). Indicates which vertical builder has contracted or taken ownership of the finished lot; used for operational segment reporting and cost-to-builder attribution.

LotNo.: Lot number within the phase (integer; e.g., 1, 2, 3). Normalized to canonical_lot_number (strip leading zeros, drop .0 decimal suffix); combined with Project and Phase for the 3-tuple lot identity key.
LotArea(sqft): Lot size in square feet (integer; e.g., 6,129 sqft). Used in density/acreage calculations and lot-value allocation models; informs builder feasibility and cost-per-lot analysis.

LotCount: Count of buildable units on the lot (typically 1 for single-family; >1 for multi-family or cluster). Denominator for per-unit cost and lot-mix variance; affects collateral lot-count rollups at phase and project grain.

SHComb: Synthetic hash / concatenation key combining Project, Phase, and LotNo. (e.g., 'Arrowhead Springs1231'). Operational convenience field for cross-system lot matching; not authoritative (derived from other columns).

HorzPurchase: Date horizontal developer acquired or contracted the raw land parcel. Marks the start of land development lifecycle; input to lot-state waterfall (LAND_OWNED stage). Sourced from internal land acquisition contracts.

HorzMDA: Mutual Development Agreement execution date. Documents partner operational handoff point; used to track when co-development obligations commence. Intermediate milestone in horizontal lifecycle.

HorzPrelimPlat: Preliminary plat approval / submitted date. Regulatory milestone in horizontal development; marks progression toward site-plan certainty. Input to lot-state HORIZONTAL_IN_PROGRESS determination.
HorzFinalPlat: Final plat recorded date. Legal completion of horizontal development; lot becomes legally subdivided and ready for vertical purchase. Triggers FINISHED_LOT state transition.

HorzStart: Horizontal infrastructure work commencement date. Marks beginning of on-site development (roads, utilities, grading). Paired with HorzEnd for infrastructure-duration calculations and phase-activity reporting.

HorzEnd: Horizontal infrastructure work completion date. Signals readiness for vertical home construction; paired with HorzStart to measure infrastructure cycle time. Used in operational progress tracking.

HorzFinInv: Horizontal phase final invoice amount (optional, typically null). Captures total horizontal cost invoice if available; rarely populated. When present, ties to GL posting for phase-level cost reconciliation.

HorzRecord: Horizontal final plat recording date (typically mirrors HorzFinalPlat). Administrative recording with county; marks formal subdivision creation. Input to finished-lot state and collateral lot-count qualification.

HorzWEnter: Water / utility entry date (optional, typically null). Denotes when utility infrastructure is energized or activated. When populated, refines horizontal-completion milestone; sparse coverage.
HorzWExit: Water / utility exit or punch-list date (optional, typically null). Signals final utility remediation or closeout. Sparse; used in detailed operational scheduling where available.

HorzContract: Horizontal contract amount or final cost (optional, typically null). Budgeted or actual horizontal cost; when populated, reconciles against GL postings. Sparse; trust GL as primary cost source.

HorzSale: Horizontal lot sale / transfer to vertical builder date. Documents when finished lot ownership transfers from horizontal developer to vertical builder. Marks the boundary between horizontal and vertical phases.
VertPurchase: Vertical builder's lot purchase / contract date. Marks VERTICAL_PURCHASED state in lot waterfall; often post-HorzSale but may pre-date it. Input to lot-lifecycle stage determination.

VertStart: Vertical home construction start date (nullable). When populated, signals VERTICAL_IN_PROGRESS state; null treated as unstarted (e.g., planned future start). Used in construction-progress reporting and completion-percentage estimation.

VertCO: Vertical certificate of occupancy date (as string; e.g., '2025-10-15T00:00:00' or '00:00:00' for null). Marks home construction completion and occupancy readiness; maps to VERTICAL_COMPLETE state. Stored as string due to mixed null-representation.

VertSale: Vertical home sale date (as string; '00:00:00' indicates null). Date home is sold to end buyer; when present, transitions lot to SOLD_NOT_CLOSED. Sourced from internal sales tracking; correlates with inventory closing report.

VertClose: Vertical home closing / settlement date (as string; '00:00:00' indicates null). Final escrow closing and buyer funds receipt; marks CLOSED state and end of operational lifecycle. When ≤ as_of_date, lot is financially closed.
Line Type: Denotes row classification in the GL detail (e.g., '-- detail') to distinguish transaction-level rows from summary or header rows in the Vertical Financials ledger.
Company: Numeric company code (1000 for BCPD), used as a foreign key to identify the legal entity posting the transaction in the chart of accounts.
Company Name: Full legal-entity name ('Building Construction Partners, LLC'); corresponds to Company code. Primary authority for canonical_entity mapping per source_to_field_map.md § Identity.
Division: Numeric division code (0 for Corporate); sub-organization within the company for cost tracking and reporting hierarchy.
Division Name: Text name of the division ('Corporate'); used for GL reporting and organizational roll-up in financial statements.
Account: Legacy 4-digit GL account code (1535, 1540, 1547 are the only BCPD VF asset accounts); identifies the account type (Permits, Construction, etc.) in the Vertical Financials chart.
Account Name: Descriptive label for the account (e.g., 'Permits and Fees'); used to categorize cost and support mapping to canonical_cost_category per source_to_field_map.md § Financial.
Sub-Ledger: Subledger account code if applicable; sparse in VF (mostly null). Used for detailed cost allocation when present.
Sub-Ledger Name: Text name of the subledger; sparse in VF (mostly null). Provides detail-level cost classification when subledger account is populated.
Account Type: Classification of the account ('Asset', 'Liability', etc.); filters transaction scope. VF v2.1 uses Asset-side only (debits).
Account Group: Functional grouping ('Balance Sheet', 'P&L'); used to determine whether a row is capitalized vs. expensed and for GL reconciliation.
Manual RE Postings: Manual real-estate adjustments if posted; sparse in VF (mostly null). When populated, adjusts the transaction amount for depreciation or fair-value restatement.
Fiscal Year: Fiscal year of the transaction (2018–2025 range for BCPD); used to organize GL by year and to validate posting-date alignment.
First Day of FY: Start date of the fiscal year; sparse in VF (mostly null). Used internally for period-matching validation in GL systems.
Last Day of FY: End date of the fiscal year; sparse in VF (mostly null). Used for fiscal-period boundary enforcement in GL closing.
Opening Balance Date: As-of date of the opening balance for the account in the period; sparse (mostly null). Useful for balance-sheet reconciliation.
Opening Balance: Starting balance of the account at the period's opening date; sparse (mostly null). Combined with Debit/Credit to derive Closing Balance.
Debit amount posted to the account; sparse in VF (mostly null for asset accounts where the Amount field is the primary debit carrier).
Credit amount posted to the account; sparse in VF (mostly null). Used to reverse or offset debit entries.
Closing Balance: Ending balance of the account at the period's close; sparse (mostly null). Used for balance-sheet tieout and period reconciliation.
Closing Balance Date: As-of date of the closing balance; sparse (mostly null). Anchors the period-end snapshot for GL reconciliation.

Posting Date: Transaction posting date from the Vertical Financials GL extract (2018–2025 BCPD cost basis). Timestamp indicates when the GL entry was recorded; ranges 2018-06 onwards. Used to filter historical cost windows and reconcile against GL posting periods.
Posting Date FY: Fiscal year derived from Posting Date; used to confirm alignment with Fiscal Year field and flag period mismatches.

Memo 1: Narrative field from Vertical Financials GL, typically capturing invoice or transaction reference (e.g., 'Inv:Bld Permit HB29:Alpine City', 'Inv:30403:Accena Group'). Part of the 9-field deduplication key for DataRails row-multiplication dedup logic; preserved for traceability and vendor/cost-reason drilldown.
Memo 2: Secondary memo field; sparse in VF (mostly null). Used for additional narrative when Memo 1 space is exhausted.
Amount: Signed transaction amount in USD (positive = debit, negative = credit); one-sided asset-only feed so typically positive. Primary cost datum for per-lot/per-phase rollups in v2.1.
Str: Structure code ('H' = Horizontal, 'V' = Vertical); indicates which development phase the cost belongs to for cost-date classification.
OUnit: Operating unit code ('HB' for horizontal build); used to segment costs by operational responsibility and to route to phase/lot allocation.

Project identifier from the Vertical Financials GL extract (2018–2025 BCPD cost basis). Maps to `canonical_project` via the project-code crosswalk; 'SctLot' denotes Scattered Lots (inferred-unknown identity, $6.55M). Never null; 100% fill on BCPD VF rows.
Lot identifier within the project; numeric (e.g., 29). When populated (100% in BCPD VF), joins to canonical_lot_number via vf_lot_code_decoder_v1 to resolve phase+lot pairs.
Major account code (e.g., 1905); sub-classification within Account for cost tracking. Used alongside Minor to form a secondary account dimension.
Minor account code (0 in samples); further subdivides the Major code. Together (Major, Minor) provide a detailed cost-center key.
Group OUnit: Group-level operating unit code ('HB'); aggregation level above OUnit for rollup and reporting hierarchy.
Group No: Numeric group identifier (e.g., 8084); joins to Group OUnit to identify the cost group. Used for phase/community aggregation.
Unit No: Numeric unit identifier within the group (e.g., 1, 48, 466); further subdivides Group No for lot-level or sub-phase tracking.
Line No: Line number within a multi-line transaction (1 in samples); used to order detail rows within a single posting.
Trans No: Transaction number for audit trail (0 in samples); unique identifier for reversals and related-entry tracking in the GL.
Journal Code: Journal type code ('PJ' = Posting Journal); identifies the source system or batch type that posted the entry.
Source of the posting ('PO Match', etc.); indicates the origin (purchase order match, manual entry, etc.) for transaction provenance and audit.
Created By: User ID of the person who created the entry ('SusanP', etc.); used for audit trail and approval workflow tracking.
Create Date: Timestamp when the entry was created in the GL system; used to establish the entry's creation sequence and version history.
Approved By: User ID of the approver ('terryw', etc.); indicates who authorized the posting and is responsible for accuracy.
Approval Date: Date the entry was approved and posted; gates the entry into the GL and establishes the official posting timestamp.
Approval Time: Time of day the entry was approved (HHMM format, e.g., 1147 = 11:47 AM); adds precision to the approval sequence.
Refresh Date: Timestamp when the VF export was refreshed ('Feb 11 2026 1:12PM'); indicates data currency and is used to detect stale snapshots.
active: Active flag ('Y' = active, 'N' = inactive); determines whether the row is included in GL rollups or is archived/reversed.

Project: Community identifier (e.g., 'HC' for Harmony or similar shorthand), derived from the Collateral workbook's master lot registry. Used to group lots by project for collateral and address tracking purposes.
Phase or plat identifier within the project (e.g., 'L'), normalized per whitespace and casing conventions. Primary key component for lot identification alongside Project and Lot number.
Lot number within the phase, stored as integer. Combined with Project and Phase to form the lot identifier; maps to canonical_lot_number in the operational state.

datasources.description: Unnamed: 3: Street address of the lot, e.g. '1901 N CREST ROAD SARATOGA SPRINGS, UT 84045'. Sourced from property records or collateral documentation; nullable for lots without recorded addresses.

Unnamed: 4: Placeholder or auxiliary numeric field with no sample values populated. Data type float suggests a potential numeric field (e.g., address number or parcel identifier) that is not currently in use.
NEW STAR PROJECT CODE: NewStar ERP system project code, nullable and unpopulated in samples. Would facilitate integration with NewStar accounting or property-management systems if wired.
AComb: Concatenated identifier combining project and lot (e.g., 'HCL1' from Project 'HC' + Phase 'L' + Lot 1). Used as a shorthand key for internal tracking and validation across workbook tabs.
PreviousACommLot: Prior-period version of the AComb identifier, capturing the lot's project+phase+lot encoding from a previous snapshot or data refresh. Enables change tracking between periods.
LotAdj: Lot adjustment factor or modifier, nullable and unpopulated in samples. Likely used to flag or quantify post-hoc lot numbering corrections or redistricting events.
CurrentACommLot: Current-period AComb-style identifier; mirrors AComb in most cases, used for validation that the lot identity has not drifted between current and prior periods.
ACheck: Validation flag (numeric, nullable) comparing PreviousACommLot to CurrentACommLot. Null or 0 when the lot identity is stable; non-null when a discrepancy is detected.
CCommTrim: Trimmed community/project code (e.g., 'HC'), derived from Project after removing whitespace. Feeds into composite identifiers and cross-workbook referencing.
CLot: Lot number copy or derived field; echoes the Lot column. Used redundantly in validation chains or composite-key construction.
CurrCComb: Current-period community+lot composite (e.g., 'HC1' from CCommTrim 'HC' + CLot 1). Simpler than AComb (omits phase) for quick project+lot rollups.
PrevCComb: Prior-period community+lot composite identifier, nullable. Enables phase-independent lot-identity tracking across periods.
ACheck.1: Literal validation string ('Check') marking that the lot identity (project+lot) has been audited between periods. Static 'Check' value signals a passed validation gate.
NSLot: NewStar lot number as maintained in the NewStar accounting system. Allows reconciliation of the collateral worksheet's lot identifiers against NewStar's system-of-record lot codes.
CurrNSComb: Current-period NewStar composite identifier, numeric (e.g., 1, 2, 3), likely CCommTrim project code + NSLot number concatenated or encoded numerically.
PrevNSComb: Prior-period NewStar composite identifier, enabling auditing of whether the lot's NewStar encoding has changed across collateral report cycles.
NSCheck: Validation flag comparing PrevNSComb to CurrNSComb; null when NewStar codes are stable, non-null when a NewStar remapping or reallocation is detected.

CurrMRcuParent Name: MoneyCorp (or similar bank/escrow system) parent record name or code in the current period, nullable and unpopulated in samples. Flags which bank account or escrow roll-up owns the lot.
PrevMRcuParent Name: Prior-period escrow/bank parent identifier, enabling audit of escrow-account reassignments or transfers between periods.
MRcuCheck: Validation flag for escrow-parent stability; null when CurrMRcuParent equals PrevMRcuParent, flagged when an escrow reassignment has occurred.
SHProject: Placeholder project code field (hardcoded 'xx' in samples), likely reserved for integration with a secondary system (e.g., Showingtime, HomeAdvisor) or internal staging code.
SHPhase: Placeholder phase code for the secondary system (hardcoded 'xx'), mirroring SHProject structure for lot-identity completeness if the secondary system is ever wired.
SHLot: Placeholder lot code for the secondary system (hardcoded 'xx'), completing the project+phase+lot triple for the secondary system if integration occurs.
CurrSHComb: Current-period secondary-system composite identifier (e.g., 'xxxxxx' from SHProject + SHPhase + SHLot), formatted as a 6-char or similar string; reserved but not in active use.
PrevSHComb: Prior-period secondary-system composite, nullable and unpopulated, enables future tracking of secondary-system lot remappings once that system is active.
SHCheck: Validation string ('Check') for secondary-system lot-identity consistency; static 'Check' value signals readiness for secondary-system integration or audit flag.
Community name (e.g. Harmony, Parkway Fields) identifying the residential development project. Used to link pricing tiers to operational project context.
Product Type: Residential product category: SFR (single-family), TH (townhome), or MF (multifamily). Defines the pricing tier and cost structure within a community.
Permit and fees cost per unit in USD, typically $18,000–$21,000. Component of the base cost structure used in internal offer pricing models.
Base Costs: Direct construction cost per unit in USD (e.g. $164,000–$227,000 range). Excludes options and represents the minimum buildable cost for the product type.
Option Cost: Average or typical add-on cost per unit in USD for customer upgrades or options. Typically $3,000–$20,000; used in sales pricing and margin modeling.
Total: Sum of Permit + Base Costs + Option Cost per unit in USD. Represents the all-in per-unit cost used for internal offer pricing and margin calculations.
Turn #: Sequential turn identifier (integer) tracking interaction order within a single Claude conversation session about the Collateral Dec2025 workbook.
Date: Calendar date (timestamp) when the turn occurred; used to correlate conversation sessions across multiple days of workbook refinement.
